(Inagi)}} [[JA:大麻止乃豆乃天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11442032}} == Japanese content == {{独自研究|date=2016年12月28日 (水) 05:25 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 大麻止乃豆乃天神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|270px]]
拝殿（2009年5月） |所在地 = {{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|稲城市|Q317847}}{{jalink|大丸 (稲城市)|Q30923766|大丸}}847 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|櫛真智命|Q108039939}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}論社・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月|Q124}}第1土曜日の翌日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=大麻止乃
豆乃天神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大麻止乃豆乃天神社'''（おおまとのつのてんじんしゃ、おおまとのつのあまつかみのやしろ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|稲城市|Q317847}}{{jalink|大丸 (稲城市)|Q30923766|大丸}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 ===祭神=== ; 主殿 * {{jalink|櫛真智命|Q108039939}}（くしまちのみこと） : 天曽己多智命の父とされる神早川純三郎「度会氏系図」『系図綜覧』巻二、国書刊行会、1915年、396頁。。『延喜式神名帳』に記載されている{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|十市郡|Q11405036}}の天香山坐櫛眞命神社（現・{{jalink|天香山神社|Q98082987}}、奈良県{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}の{{jalink|天香久山|Q11443780}}北麓に鎮座）に「元名 大麻等乃智神」との注記があることから、当社も天香山神社と同じ祭神であろうと考えられたものである。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}までは丸宮明神（まるみやみょうじん）と称していたが、これは「麻止乃宮明神」が転じたものという説がある{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 大丸村}}。 === 歴史 === [[Image:Torii of Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|鳥居 (2009年5月)]] [[Image:Komainu in Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|狛犬。「{{jalink|天保|Q1369045}}十二{{jalink|辛丑|Q904956}}年六月吉祥日」（{{jalink|1841年|Q7622}}）と彫られている。 (2009年5月)]] [[Image:View from Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|境内からの{{jalink|多摩川|Q1063006}}中流域の{{jalink|沖積地}}の眺め。多摩川にかかる{{jalink|稲城大橋|Q11596545}}が見える。(2009年5月)]] ごく小さな社であり由緒ははっきりしないが、『{{jalink|式内社調査報告}}』では、『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載される{{jalink|武蔵国|Q907398}}{{jalink|多摩郡|Q11430789}}「大麻止乃豆乃天神社」が当社であるとしている。しかし、式内・大麻止乃豆乃天神社については東京都{{jalink|青梅市|Q237683}}の{{jalink|武蔵御嶽神社|Q6587276}}も{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}となっており、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}初年には武蔵御嶽神社が大麻止乃豆乃天神社と称していた。 なお「おおまとのつ」とは「大きく丸い平地にある船着き場」という意味ではないかと考えられている稲城市教育委員会編『稲城の地名と旧道』73頁。。実際、この神社の神域に隣接する{{jalink|普門禅庵}}の前の低地の旧地名は「イリガタ」であり、天神山と城山（{{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}の砦の遺構あり）に挟まれた{{jalink|古多摩川}}の{{jalink|潟湖}}であった。 この神社が立地する稲城市大丸という地名も、「大きく丸い平地」という意味であるとされるが、この地は古代から{{jalink|多摩川|Q1063006}}の渡河点の一つ（{{jalink|府中街道|Q11486060}}と川崎街道がここで合流する）であり、{{jalink|津|Q11555884}}があったと考えるのは妥当であろう。ちなみに前出の城山も、多摩川の渡河点を押さえる目的で築かれたと考えられている。 === 境内社 === '''{{jalink|秋葉神社|Q11595841}}'''{{efn|『{{jalink|新編武蔵風土記稿|Q11503943}}』の丸宮社の項に「末社 秋葉社」の記述がある{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 大丸村}}。}} :[祭神] {{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火之迦具土大神}} '''{{jalink|津島神社|Q705136}}''' :[祭神] {{jalink|建速須佐之男命}} :[行事] 例大祭（7月海の日） '''{{jalink|稲荷神|Q719665|稲荷神社}}''' × 2社 :[祭神] {{jalink|倉稲魂命}} :[行事] 初午祭（2月上旬） '''{{jalink|白山信仰|Q11579557|白山神社}}''' :[祭神] {{jalink|菊理姫命}}（くくりひめのみこと） :[祭神] {{jalink|伊弉諾命}}（いざなぎのみこと） :[祭神] {{jalink|伊弉冊命}}（いざなみのみこと） '''{{jalink|神明神社|Q11590310}}''' :[祭神] {{jalink|天照大御神}} File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Tsushima-jinja.jpg|津島神社 File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner1.jpg|合社殿（白山神社,神明神社,稲荷神社） File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Akiba-jinja.jpg|秋葉神社 File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷神社 File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner2.jpg|境内社 === 祭事 === * 元旦祭 {{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}} * 初午祭 {{jalink|2月|Q109}}上旬 * 津島神社{{jalink|例祭|Q11385469}} {{jalink|7月|Q121}}海の日 * 風祭 {{jalink|9月1日|Q2859}} * {{jalink|多摩丘陵|Q1502721}}中部の北辺に位置し、境内からは{{jalink|多摩川低地|Q131910796|多摩川中流域の沖積地}}が一望出来る。境内の裏山はかつては城山と呼ばれ、中世には大丸城という{{jalink|山城|Q15710038}}が築かれていたが、現在は{{jalink|多摩ニュータウン|Q7680510}}{{jalink|向陽台 (稲城市)|Q11416047|向陽台}}住区となっている。参道の両脇には円照寺、直心庵という仏教寺院があり、境内の北側にはやはり普門禅庵という仏教寺院がある。 === 脚注 === === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * {{cite (Inagi)}} [[JA:大麻止乃豆乃天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11442032}} == Japanese content == {{独自研究|date=2016年12月28日 (水) 05:25 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 大麻止乃豆乃天神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|270px]]
拝殿（2009年5月） |所在地 = {{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|稲城市|Q317847}}{{jalink|大丸 (稲城市)|Q30923766|大丸}}847 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|櫛真智命|Q108039939}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}論社・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月|Q124}}第1土曜日の翌日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=大麻止乃
豆乃天神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大麻止乃豆乃天神社'''（おおまとのつのてんじんしゃ、おおまとのつのあまつかみのやしろ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|稲城市|Q317847}}{{jalink|大丸 (稲城市)|Q30923766|大丸}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 ===祭神=== ; 主殿 * {{jalink|櫛真智命|Q108039939}}（くしまちのみこと） : 天曽己多智命の父とされる神早川純三郎「度会氏系図」『系図綜覧』巻二、国書刊行会、1915年、396頁。。『延喜式神名帳』に記載されている{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|十市郡|Q11405036}}の天香山坐櫛眞命神社（現・{{jalink|天香山神社|Q98082987}}、奈良県{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}の{{jalink|天香久山|Q11443780}}北麓に鎮座）に「元名 大麻等乃智神」との注記があることから、当社も天香山神社と同じ祭神であろうと考えられたものである。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}までは丸宮明神（まるみやみょうじん）と称していたが、これは「麻止乃宮明神」が転じたものという説がある{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 大丸村}}。 === 歴史 === [[Image:Torii of Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|鳥居 (2009年5月)]] [[Image:Komainu in Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|狛犬。「{{jalink|天保|Q1369045}}十二{{jalink|辛丑|Q904956}}年六月吉祥日」（{{jalink|1841年|Q7622}}）と彫られている。 (2009年5月)]] [[Image:View from Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|境内からの{{jalink|多摩川|Q1063006}}中流域の{{jalink|沖積地}}の眺め。多摩川にかかる{{jalink|稲城大橋|Q11596545}}が見える。(2009年5月)]] ごく小さな社であり由緒ははっきりしないが、『{{jalink|式内社調査報告}}』では、『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載される{{jalink|武蔵国|Q907398}}{{jalink|多摩郡|Q11430789}}「大麻止乃豆乃天神社」が当社であるとしている。しかし、式内・大麻止乃豆乃天神社については東京都{{jalink|青梅市|Q237683}}の{{jalink|武蔵御嶽神社|Q6587276}}も{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}となっており、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}初年には武蔵御嶽神社が大麻止乃豆乃天神社と称していた。 なお「おおまとのつ」とは「大きく丸い平地にある船着き場」という意味ではないかと考えられている稲城市教育委員会編『稲城の地名と旧道』73頁。。実際、この神社の神域に隣接する{{jalink|普門禅庵}}の前の低地の旧地名は「イリガタ」であり、天神山と城山（{{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}の砦の遺構あり）に挟まれた{{jalink|古多摩川}}の{{jalink|潟湖}}であった。 この神社が立地する稲城市大丸という地名も、「大きく丸い平地」という意味であるとされるが、この地は古代から{{jalink|多摩川|Q1063006}}の渡河点の一つ（{{jalink|府中街道|Q11486060}}と川崎街道がここで合流する）であり、{{jalink|津|Q11555884}}があったと考えるのは妥当であろう。ちなみに前出の城山も、多摩川の渡河点を押さえる目的で築かれたと考えられている。 === 境内社 === '''{{jalink|秋葉神社|Q11595841}}'''{{efn|『{{jalink|新編武蔵風土記稿|Q11503943}}』の丸宮社の項に「末社 秋葉社」の記述がある{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 大丸村}}。}} :[祭神] {{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火之迦具土大神}} '''{{jalink|津島神社|Q705136}}''' :[祭神] {{jalink|建速須佐之男命}} :[行事] 例大祭（7月海の日） '''{{jalink|稲荷神|Q719665|稲荷神社}}''' × 2社 :[祭神] {{jalink|倉稲魂命}} :[行事] 初午祭（2月上旬） '''{{jalink|白山信仰|Q11579557|白山神社}}''' :[祭神] {{jalink|菊理姫命}}（くくりひめのみこと） :[祭神] {{jalink|伊弉諾命}}（いざなぎのみこと） :[祭神] {{jalink|伊弉冊命}}（いざなみのみこと） '''{{jalink|神明神社|Q11590310}}''' :[祭神] {{jalink|天照大御神}} File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Tsushima-jinja.jpg|津島神社 File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner1.jpg|合社殿（白山神社,神明神社,稲荷神社） File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Akiba-jinja.jpg|秋葉神社 File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷神社 File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner2.jpg|境内社 === 祭事 === * 元旦祭 {{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}} * 初午祭 {{jalink|2月|Q109}}上旬 * 津島神社{{jalink|例祭|Q11385469}} {{jalink|7月|Q121}}海の日 * 風祭 {{jalink|9月1日|Q2859}} * 大麻止乃豆乃天神社例大祭 ** 宵宮祭 {{jalink|10月|Q124}}第一土曜日 ** 例大祭 10月翌日曜日 * 七五三祝い {{jalink|11月|Q125}}中旬 * 除夜祭 {{jalink|12月31日|Q2912}}深夜 === 現地情報 === ;所在地 *{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|稲城市|Q317847}}{{jalink|大丸 (稲城市)|Q30923766|大丸}}847 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：JR{{jalink|南武線|Q1366263}}{{jalink|南多摩駅|Q5366901}}下車後、徒歩約8分（南へ約600m） ;周辺 {{jalink|多摩丘陵|Q1502721}}中部の北辺に位置し、境内からは{{jalink|多摩川低地|Q131910796|多摩川中流域の沖積地}}が一望出来る。境内の裏山はかつては城山と呼ばれ、中世には大丸城という{{jalink|山城|Q15710038}}が築かれていたが、現在は{{jalink|多摩ニュータウン|Q7680510}}{{jalink|向陽台 (稲城市)|Q11416047|向陽台}}住区となっている。参道の両脇には円照寺、直心庵という仏教寺院があり、境内の北側にはやはり普門禅庵という仏教寺院がある。 === 脚注 === === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * {{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=巻ノ95多磨郡ノ7|chapter=大丸村 丸宮社|id={{NDLJP|763989/50}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 大丸村}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:おおまとのつのてんしんしや}} == (Inagi)}} [[JA:大麻止乃豆乃天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11442032}} == Japanese content == {{Original research|date=December 28, 2016 (Wed) 05:25 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine |Image = [[File:Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (May 2009) |Location = {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490}}{{jalink|Inagi City|Q317847}}{{jalink|Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|Ōmaru}} 847 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287}} (theoretical identification); former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Established = |Main hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Other name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = The Sunday following the first Saturday of {{jalink|October|Q124}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Ōmatono
Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine''' (おおまとのつのてんじんしゃ; also read おおまとのつのあまつかみのやしろ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|Ōmaru}}, {{jalink|Inagi City|Q317847}}, {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490}}. Its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main sanctuary * {{jalink|Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939}} (くしまちのみこと) : A deity said to be the father of Ame-no-Sokotachi no MikotoJunzaburō Hayakawa, “Watarai-shi Keizu,” *Keizu Sōran*, vol. 2, Kokushokankōkai, 1915, p. 396.. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the entry for the Ame-no-Kagu-yama-ni-masu Kushimachi no Mikoto Shrine in {{jalink|Tōichi District|Q11405036}}, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} (present-day {{jalink|Amekaguyama Shrine|Q98082987}}, located on the northern foothills of {{jalink|Mt. Amekaguyama|Q11443780}} in {{jalink|Kashihara City|Q496319}}, Nara Prefecture) includes the note “former name: Ōmatonochi no Kami.” From this, it has been considered that this shrine enshrines the same deity as Amekaguyama Shrine. Until the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the shrine was called Marumiya Myōjin, which is said to be a corruption of “Matono-no-miya Myōjin”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}. === History === [[Image:Torii of Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Torii gate (May 2009)]] [[Image:Komainu in Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Komainu. Inscribed “An auspicious day in the sixth month of the twelfth year of {{jalink|Tenpō|Q1369045}}, {{jalink|Kanoto-ushi|Q904956}}” ({{jalink|1841|Q7622}}). (May 2009)]] [[Image:View from Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the precincts over the {{jalink|alluvial plain}} of the middle reaches of the {{jalink|Tama River|Q1063006}}. {{jalink|Inagi Bridge|Q11596545}} can be seen spanning the Tama River. (May 2009)]] Although it is a very small shrine and its origins are unclear, the *{{jalink|Report on Shikinaisha Investigations}}* identifies this shrine as the “Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine” listed in the *{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}* for {{jalink|Tama District|Q11430789}}, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}. However, regarding the Shikinaisha Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine, {{jalink|Musashi Mitake Shrine|Q6587276}} in {{jalink|Ōme City|Q237683}}, Tokyo, is also considered a {{jalink|theoretical identification|Q135022904}}, and in the early {{jalink|Meiji era|Q3125096}} Musashi Mitake Shrine was known as Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine. The name “Ōmatono-tsu” is thought to mean “a landing place on a large, round, flat plain”Inagi City Board of Education (ed.), *Inagi no Chimei to Kyūdō*, p. 73.. In fact, the former place name of the lowland in front of {{jalink|Fumon Zen’an}} adjacent to the shrine precincts was “Irigata,” and it was a {{jalink|lagoon}} of the {{jalink|Old Tama River}} sandwiched between Tenjin-yama and Shiro-yama (which contains remains of a {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}} fort). The place name Ōmaru in Inagi City, where this shrine stands, is also said to mean “large, round, flat plain.” This area has been one of the river-crossing points of the {{jalink|Tama River|Q1063006}} since ancient times (where the {{jalink|Fuchū Highway|Q11486060}} and the Kawasaki Highway converge), so it is reasonable to think that there was a {{jalink|river port|Q11555884}} here. Incidentally, the aforementioned Shiro-yama is also thought to have been built to control the Tama River crossing. === Subsidiary shrines === '''{{jalink|Akiba Shrine|Q11595841}}'''{{efn|The section on Marumiya Shrine in the *{{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|Q11503943}}* includes the note “subsidiary shrine: Akiba Shrine”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}.}} :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Hi-no-Kagutsuchi Ōkami}} '''{{jalink|Tsushima Shrine|Q705136}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Takesusanoo no Mikoto}} :[Event] Annual grand festival (Marine Day in July) '''{{jalink|Inari Shrine|Q719665}}''' × 2 :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} :[Event] Hatsu-uma Festival (early February) '''{{jalink|Hakusan Shrine|Q11579557}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Kikuri-hime no Mikoto}} (くくりひめのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Izanagi no Mikoto}} (いざなぎのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Izanami no Mikoto}} (いざなみのみこと) '''{{jalink|Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami}} File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Tsushima-jinja.jpg|Tsushima Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner1.jpg|Combined shrine hall (Hakusan Shrine, Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine) File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Akiba-jinja.jpg|Akiba Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner2.jpg|Subsidiary shrines === Festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival {{jalink|January 1|Q2150}} * Hatsu-uma Festival (early {{jalink|February|Q109}}) * Tsushima Shrine {{jalink|annual festival|Q11385469}} ({{jalink|July|Q121}}, Marine Day) * Kaze Festival {{jalink|September 1|Q2859}} * Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine Annual Grand Festival ** Eve Festival: first Saturday of {{jalink|October|Q124}} ** Grand Festival: the following Sunday * Shichi-Go-San celebration (mid {{jalink|November|Q125}}) * New Year’s Eve Festival (late night of {{jalink|December 31|Q2912}}) === On-site information === ;Address * 847 {{jalink|Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|Ōmaru}}, {{jalink|Inagi City|Q317847}}, {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490}} ;Access * Nearest station: About an 8-minute walk (approx. 600 m south) from {{jalink|Minami-Tama Station|Q5366901}} on the JR {{jalink|Nambu Line|Q1366263}} ;Surroundings Located on the northern edge of the central {{jalink|Tama Hills|Q1502721}}, the shrine offers a panoramic view from its precincts of the {{jalink|Tama River lowland|Q131910796|alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River}}. The wooded hill behind the precincts was formerly called Shiro-yama; in the medieval period, a mountain castle known as Ōmaru Castle, a {{jalink|yamajiro|Q15710038}}, was built there. Today, the area forms part of the {{jalink|Tama New Town|Q7680510}} {{jalink|Kōyōdai (Inagi City)|Q11416047|Kōyōdai}} residential district. Along both sides of the approach to the shrine are the Buddhist temples Enshō-ji and Jikishin-an, and to the north of the precincts is another Buddhist temple, Fumon Zen’an. === Notes === === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Further reading === * {{cite book|language=ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Vol. 95, Tama District no. 7|chapter=Ōmaru Village, Marumiya Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/50}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884–6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Omatonotsunotenjinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:稲城市の建築物]] [[Category:稲城市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine''' (おおまとのつのてんじんしゃ; also read おおまとのつのあまつかみのやしろ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|Ōmaru}}, {{jalink|Inagi City|Q317847}}, {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490}}. Its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main sanctuary * {{jalink|Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939}} (くしまちのみこと) : A deity said to be the father of Ame-no-Sokotachi no MikotoJunzaburō Hayakawa, “Watarai-shi Keizu,” *Keizu Sōran*, vol. 2, Kokushokankōkai, 1915, p. 396.. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the entry for the Ame-no-Kagu-yama-ni-masu Kushimachi no Mikoto Shrine in {{jalink|Tōichi District|Q11405036}}, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} (present-day {{jalink|Amekaguyama Shrine|Q98082987}}, located on the northern foothills of {{jalink|Mt. Amekaguyama|Q11443780}} in {{jalink|Kashihara City|Q496319}}, Nara Prefecture) includes the note “former name: Ōmatonochi no Kami.” From this, it has been considered that this shrine enshrines the same deity as Amekaguyama Shrine. Until the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the shrine was called Marumiya Myōjin, which is said to be a corruption of “Matono-no-miya Myōjin”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}. === History === [[Image:Torii of Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Torii gate (May 2009)]] [[Image:Komainu in Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Komainu. Inscribed “An auspicious day in the sixth month of the twelfth year of {{jalink|Tenpō|Q1369045}}, {{jalink|Kanoto-ushi|Q904956}}” ({{jalink|1841|Q7622}}). (May 2009)]] [[Image:View from Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the precincts over the {{jalink|alluvial plain}} of the middle reaches of the {{jalink|Tama River|Q1063006}}. {{jalink|Inagi Bridge|Q11596545}} can be seen spanning the Tama River. (May 2009)]] Although it is a very small shrine and its origins are unclear, the *{{jalink|Report on Shikinaisha Investigations}}* identifies this shrine as the “Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine” listed in the *{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}* for {{jalink|Tama District|Q11430789}}, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}. However, regarding the Shikinaisha Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine, {{jalink|Musashi Mitake Shrine|Q6587276}} in {{jalink|Ōme City|Q237683}}, Tokyo, is also considered a {{jalink|theoretical identification|Q135022904}}, and in the early {{jalink|Meiji era|Q3125096}} Musashi Mitake Shrine was known as Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine. The name “Ōmatono-tsu” is thought to mean “a landing place on a large, round, flat plain”Inagi City Board of Education (ed.), *Inagi no Chimei to Kyūdō*, p. 73.. In fact, the former place name of the lowland in front of {{jalink|Fumon Zen’an}} adjacent to the shrine precincts was “Irigata,” and it was a {{jalink|lagoon}} of the {{jalink|Old Tama River}} sandwiched between Tenjin-yama and Shiro-yama (which contains remains of a {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}} fort). The place name Ōmaru in Inagi City, where this shrine stands, is also said to mean “large, round, flat plain.” This area has been one of the river-crossing points of the {{jalink|Tama River|Q1063006}} since ancient times (where the {{jalink|Fuchū Highway|Q11486060}} and the Kawasaki Highway converge), so it is reasonable to think that there was a {{jalink|river port|Q11555884}} here. Incidentally, the aforementioned Shiro-yama is also thought to have been built to control the Tama River crossing. === Subsidiary shrines === '''{{jalink|Akiba Shrine|Q11595841}}'''{{efn|The section (Inagi)}} [[JA:大麻止乃豆乃天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11442032}} == Japanese content == {{Original research|date=December 28, 2016 (Wed) 05:25 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine |Image = [[File:Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (May 2009) |Location = {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490}}{{jalink|Inagi City|Q317847}}{{jalink|Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|Ōmaru}} 847 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287}} (theoretical identification); former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Established = |Main hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Other name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = The Sunday following the first Saturday of {{jalink|October|Q124}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Ōmatono
Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine''' (おおまとのつのてんじんしゃ; also read おおまとのつのあまつかみのやしろ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|Ōmaru}}, {{jalink|Inagi City|Q317847}}, {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490}}. Its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main sanctuary * {{jalink|Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939}} (くしまちのみこと) : A deity said to be the father of Ame-no-Sokotachi no MikotoJunzaburō Hayakawa, “Watarai-shi Keizu,” *Keizu Sōran*, vol. 2, Kokushokankōkai, 1915, p. 396.. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the entry for the Ame-no-Kagu-yama-ni-masu Kushimachi no Mikoto Shrine in {{jalink|Tōichi District|Q11405036}}, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} (present-day {{jalink|Amekaguyama Shrine|Q98082987}}, located on the northern foothills of {{jalink|Mt. Amekaguyama|Q11443780}} in {{jalink|Kashihara City|Q496319}}, Nara Prefecture) includes the note “former name: Ōmatonochi no Kami.” From this, it has been considered that this shrine enshrines the same deity as Amekaguyama Shrine. Until the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the shrine was called Marumiya Myōjin, which is said to be a corruption of “Matono-no-miya Myōjin”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}. === History === [[Image:Torii of Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Torii gate (May 2009)]] [[Image:Komainu in Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Komainu. Inscribed “An auspicious day in the sixth month of the twelfth year of {{jalink|Tenpō|Q1369045}}, {{jalink|Kanoto-ushi|Q904956}}” ({{jalink|1841|Q7622}}). (May 2009)]] [[Image:View from Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the precincts over the {{jalink|alluvial plain}} of the middle reaches of the {{jalink|Tama River|Q1063006}}. {{jalink|Inagi Bridge|Q11596545}} can be seen spanning the Tama River. (May 2009)]] Although it is a very small shrine and its origins are unclear, the *{{jalink|Report on Shikinaisha Investigations}}* identifies this shrine as the “Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine” listed in the *{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}* for {{jalink|Tama District|Q11430789}}, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}. However, regarding the Shikinaisha Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine, {{jalink|Musashi Mitake Shrine|Q6587276}} in {{jalink|Ōme City|Q237683}}, Tokyo, is also considered a {{jalink|theoretical identification|Q135022904}}, and in the early {{jalink|Meiji era|Q3125096}} Musashi Mitake Shrine was known as Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine. The name “Ōmatono-tsu” is thought to mean “a landing place on a large, round, flat plain”Inagi City Board of Education (ed.), *Inagi no Chimei to Kyūdō*, p. 73.. In fact, the former place name of the lowland in front of {{jalink|Fumon Zen’an}} adjacent to the shrine precincts was “Irigata,” and it was a {{jalink|lagoon}} of the {{jalink|Old Tama River}} sandwiched between Tenjin-yama and Shiro-yama (which contains remains of a {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}} fort). The place name Ōmaru in Inagi City, where this shrine stands, is also said to mean “large, round, flat plain.” This area has been one of the river-crossing points of the {{jalink|Tama River|Q1063006}} since ancient times (where the {{jalink|Fuchū Highway|Q11486060}} and the Kawasaki Highway converge), so it is reasonable to think that there was a {{jalink|river port|Q11555884}} here. Incidentally, the aforementioned Shiro-yama is also thought to have been built to control the Tama River crossing. === Subsidiary shrines === '''{{jalink|Akiba Shrine|Q11595841}}'''{{efn|The section on Marumiya Shrine in the *{{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|Q11503943}}* includes the note “subsidiary shrine: Akiba Shrine”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}.}} :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Hi-no-Kagutsuchi Ōkami}} '''{{jalink|Tsushima Shrine|Q705136}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Takesusanoo no Mikoto}} :[Event] Annual grand festival (Marine Day in July) '''{{jalink|Inari Shrine|Q719665}}''' × 2 :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} :[Event] Hatsu-uma Festival (early February) '''{{jalink|Hakusan Shrine|Q11579557}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Kikuri-hime no Mikoto}} (くくりひめのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Izanagi no Mikoto}} (いざなぎのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Izanami no Mikoto}} (いざなみのみこと) '''{{jalink|Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami}} File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Tsushima-jinja.jpg|Tsushima Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner1.jpg|Combined shrine hall (Hakusan Shrine, Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine) File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Akiba-jinja.jpg|Akiba Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner2.jpg|Subsidiary shrines === Festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival {{jalink|January 1|Q2150}} * Hatsu-uma Festival (early {{jalink|February|Q109}}) * Tsushima Shrine {{jalink|annual festival|Q11385469}} ({{jalink|July|Q121}}, Marine Day) * Kaze Festival {{jalink|September 1|Q2859}} * Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine Annual Grand Festival ** Eve Festival: first Saturday of {{jalink|October|Q124}} ** Grand Festival: the following Sunday * Shichi-Go-San celebration (mid {{jalink|November|Q125}}) * New Year’s Eve Festival (late night of {{jalink|December 31|Q2912}}) === On-site information === ;Address * 847 {{jalink|Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|Ōmaru}}, {{jalink|Inagi City|Q317847}}, {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490}} ;Access * Nearest station: About an 8-minute walk (approx. 600 m south) from {{jalink|Minami-Tama Station|Q5366901}} on the JR {{jalink|Nambu Line|Q1366263}} ;Surroundings Located on the northern edge of the central {{jalink|Tama Hills|Q1502721}}, the shrine offers a panoramic view from its precincts of the {{jalink|Tama River lowland|Q131910796|alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River}}. The wooded hill behind the precincts was formerly called Shiro-yama; in the medieval period, a mountain castle known as Ōmaru Castle, a {{jalink|yamajiro|Q15710038}}, was built there. Today, the area forms part of the {{jalink|Tama New Town|Q7680510}} {{jalink|Kōyōdai (Inagi City)|Q11416047|Kōyōdai}} residential district. Along both sides of the approach to the shrine are the Buddhist temples Enshō-ji and Jikishin-an, and to the north of the precincts is another Buddhist temple, Fumon Zen’an. === Notes === === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Further reading === * {{cite book|language=ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Vol. 95, Tama District no. 7|chapter=Ōmaru Village, Marumiya Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/50}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884–6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Omatonotsunotenjinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:稲城市の建築物]] [[Category:稲城市の歴史]]

In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the entry for the Ame-no-Kagu-yama-ni-masu Kushimachi no Mikoto Shrine in {{jalink|Tōichi District|Q11405036}}, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} (present-day {{jalink|Amekaguyama Shrine|Q98082987}}, located on the northern foothills of {{jalink|Mt. Amekaguyama|Q11443780}} in {{jalink|Kashihara City|Q496319}}, Nara Prefecture) includes the note “former name: Ōmatonochi no Kami.” From this, it has been considered that this shrine enshrines the same deity as Amekaguyama Shrine. Until the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the shrine was called Marumiya Myōjin, which is said to be a corruption of “Matono-no-miya Myōjin”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}. === History === [[Image:Torii of Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Torii gate (May 2009)]] [[Image:Komainu in Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Komainu. Inscribed “An auspicious day in the sixth month of the twelfth year of {{jalink|Tenpō|Q1369045}}, {{jalink|Kanoto-ushi|Q904956}}” ({{jalink|1841|Q7622}}). (May 2009)]] [[Image:View from Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the precincts over the {{jalink|alluvial plain}} of the middle reaches of the {{jalink|Tama River|Q1063006}}. {{jalink|Inagi Bridge|Q11596545}} can be seen spanning the Tama River. (May 2009)]] Although it is a very small shrine and its origins are unclear, the *{{jalink|Report on Shikinaisha Investigations}}* identifies this shrine as the “Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine” listed in the *{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}* for {{jalink|Tama District|Q11430789}}, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}. However, regarding the Shikinaisha Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine, {{jalink|Musashi Mitake Shrine|Q6587276}} in {{jalink|Ōme City|Q237683}}, Tokyo, is also considered a {{jalink|theoretical identification|Q135022904}}, and in the early {{jalink|Meiji era|Q3125096}} Musashi Mitake Shrine was known as Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine. The name “Ōmatono-tsu” is thought to mean “a landing place on a large, round, flat plain”Inagi City Board of Education (ed.), *Inagi no Chimei to Kyūdō*, p. 73.. In fact, the former place name of the lowland in front of {{jalink|Fumon Zen’an}} adjacent to the shrine precincts was “Irigata,” and it was a {{jalink|lagoon}} of the {{jalink|Old Tama River}} sandwiched between Tenjin-yama and Shiro-yama (which contains remains of a {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}} fort). The place name Ōmaru in Inagi City, where this shrine stands, is also said to mean “large, round, flat plain.” This area has been one of the river-crossing points of the {{jalink|Tama River|Q1063006}} since ancient times (where the {{jalink|Fuchū Highway|Q11486060}} and the Kawasaki Highway converge), so it is reasonable to think that there was a {{jalink|river port|Q11555884}} here. Incidentally, the aforementioned Shiro-yama is also thought to have been built to control the Tama River crossing. === Subsidiary shrines === '''{{jalink|Akiba Shrine|Q11595841}}'''{{efn|The section on Marumiya Shrine in the *{{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|Q11503943}}* includes the note “subsidiary shrine: Akiba Shrine”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}.}} :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Hi-no-Kagutsuchi Ōkami}} '''{{jalink|Tsushima Shrine|Q705136}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Takesusanoo no Mikoto}} :[Event] Annual grand festival (Marine Day in July) '''{{jalink|Inari Shrine|Q719665}}''' × 2 :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} :[Event] Hatsu-uma Festival (early February) '''{{jalink|Hakusan Shrine|Q11579557}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Kikuri-hime no Mikoto}} (くくりひめのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Izanagi no Mikoto}} (いざなぎのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Izanami no Mikoto}} (いざなみのみこと) '''{{jalink|Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami}} File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Tsushima-jinja.jpg|Tsushima Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner1.jpg|Combined shrine hall (Hakusan Shrine, Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine) File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Akiba-jinja.jpg|Akiba Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner2.jpg|Subsidiary shrines === Festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival {{jalink|January 1|Q2150}} * Hatsu-uma Festival (early {{jalink|February|Q109}}) * Tsushima Shrine {{jalink|annual festival|Q11385469}} ({{jalink|July|Q121}}, Marine Day) * Kaze Festival {{jalink|September 1|Q2859}} * Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine Annual Grand Festival ** Eve Festival: first Saturday of {{jalink|October|Q124}} ** Grand Festival: the following Sunday * Shichi-Go-San celebration (mid {{jalink|November|Q125}}) * New Year’s Eve Festival (late night of {{jalink|December 31|Q2912}}) === On-site information === ;Address * 847 {{jalink|Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|Ōmaru}}, {{jalink|Inagi City|Q317847}}, {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490}} ;Access * Nearest station: About an 8-minute walk (approx. 600 m south) from {{jalink|Minami-Tama Station|Q5366901}} on the JR {{jalink|Nambu Line|Q1366263}} ;Surroundings Located on the northern edge of the central {{jalink|Tama Hills|Q1502721}}, the shrine offers a panoramic view from its precincts of the {{jalink|Tama River lowland|Q131910796|alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River}}. The wooded hill behind the precincts was formerly called Shiro-yama; in the medieval period, a mountain castle known as Ōmaru Castle, a {{jalink|yamajiro|Q15710038}}, was built there. Today, the area forms part of the {{jalink|Tama New Town|Q7680510}} {{jalink|Kōyōdai (Inagi City)|Q11416047|Kōyōdai}} residential district. Along both sides of the approach to the shrine are the Buddhist temples Enshō-ji and Jikishin-an, and to the north of the precincts is another Buddhist temple, Fumon Zen’an. === Notes === === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Further reading === * {{cite book|language=ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Vol. 95, Tama District no. 7|chapter=Ōmaru Village, Marumiya Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/50}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884–6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Omatonotsunotenjinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:稲城市の建築物]] [[Category:稲城市の歴史]]

Its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main sanctuary * {{jalink|Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939}} (くしまちのみこと) : A deity said to be the father of Ame-no-Sokotachi no MikotoJunzaburō Hayakawa, “Watarai-shi Keizu,” *Keizu Sōran*, vol. 2, Kokushokankōkai, 1915, p. 396.. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the entry for the Ame-no-Kagu-yama-ni-masu Kushimachi no Mikoto Shrine in {{jalink|Tōichi District|Q11405036}}, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} (present-day {{jalink|Amekaguyama Shrine|Q98082987}}, located on the northern foothills of {{jalink|Mt. (Inagi)}} [[JA:大麻止乃豆乃天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11442032}} == Japanese content == {{Original research|date=December 28, 2016 (Wed) 05:25 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine |Image = [[File:Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (May 2009) |Location = {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490}}{{jalink|Inagi City|Q317847}}{{jalink|Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|Ōmaru}} 847 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287}} (theoretical identification); former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Established = |Main hall = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Other name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = The Sunday following the first Saturday of {{jalink|October|Q124}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Ōmatono
Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine''' (おおまとのつのてんじんしゃ; also read おおまとのつのあまつかみのやしろ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|Ōmaru}}, {{jalink|Inagi City|Q317847}}, {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490}}. Its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main sanctuary * {{jalink|Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939}} (くしまちのみこと) : A deity said to be the father of Ame-no-Sokotachi no MikotoJunzaburō Hayakawa, “Watarai-shi Keizu,” *Keizu Sōran*, vol. 2, Kokushokankōkai, 1915, p. 396.. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the entry for the Ame-no-Kagu-yama-ni-masu Kushimachi no Mikoto Shrine in {{jalink|Tōichi District|Q11405036}}, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} (present-day {{jalink|Amekaguyama Shrine|Q98082987}}, located on the northern foothills of {{jalink|Mt. Amekaguyama|Q11443780}} in {{jalink|Kashihara City|Q496319}}, Nara Prefecture) includes the note “former name: Ōmatonochi no Kami.” From this, it has been considered that this shrine enshrines the same deity as Amekaguyama Shrine. Until the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the shrine was called Marumiya Myōjin, which is said to be a corruption of “Matono-no-miya Myōjin”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}. === History === [[Image:Torii of Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Torii gate (May 2009)]] [[Image:Komainu in Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Komainu. Inscribed “An auspicious day in the sixth month of the twelfth year of {{jalink|Tenpō|Q1369045}}, {{jalink|Kanoto-ushi|Q904956}}” ({{jalink|1841|Q7622}}). (May 2009)]] [[Image:View from Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the precincts over the {{jalink|alluvial plain}} of the middle reaches of the {{jalink|Tama River|Q1063006}}. {{jalink|Inagi Bridge|Q11596545}} can be seen spanning the Tama River. (May 2009)]] Although it is a very small shrine and its origins are unclear, the *{{jalink|Report on Shikinaisha Investigations}}* identifies this shrine as the “Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine” listed in the *{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}* for {{jalink|Tama District|Q11430789}}, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}. However, regarding the Shikinaisha Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine, {{jalink|Musashi Mitake Shrine|Q6587276}} in {{jalink|Ōme City|Q237683}}, Tokyo, is also considered a {{jalink|theoretical identification|Q135022904}}, and in the early {{jalink|Meiji era|Q3125096}} Musashi Mitake Shrine was known as Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine. The name “Ōmatono-tsu” is thought to mean “a landing place on a large, round, flat plain”Inagi City Board of Education (ed.), *Inagi no Chimei to Kyūdō*, p. 73.. In fact, the former place name of the lowland in front of {{jalink|Fumon Zen’an}} adjacent to the shrine precincts was “Irigata,” and it was a {{jalink|lagoon}} of the {{jalink|Old Tama River}} sandwiched between Tenjin-yama and Shiro-yama (which contains remains of a {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}} fort). The place name Ōmaru in Inagi City, where this shrine stands, is also said to mean “large, round, flat plain.” This area has been one of the river-crossing points of the {{jalink|Tama River|Q1063006}} since ancient times (where the {{jalink|Fuchū Highway|Q11486060}} and the Kawasaki Highway converge), so it is reasonable to think that there was a {{jalink|river port|Q11555884}} here. Incidentally, the aforementioned Shiro-yama is also thought to have been built to control the Tama River crossing. === Subsidiary shrines === '''{{jalink|Akiba Shrine|Q11595841}}'''{{efn|The section on Marumiya Shrine in the *{{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|Q11503943}}* includes the note “subsidiary shrine: Akiba Shrine”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}.}} :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Hi-no-Kagutsuchi Ōkami}} '''{{jalink|Tsushima Shrine|Q705136}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Takesusanoo no Mikoto}} :[Event] Annual grand festival (Marine Day in July) '''{{jalink|Inari Shrine|Q719665}}''' × 2 :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} :[Event] Hatsu-uma Festival (early February) '''{{jalink|Hakusan Shrine|Q11579557}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Kikuri-hime no Mikoto}} (くくりひめのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Izanagi no Mikoto}} (いざなぎのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Izanami no Mikoto}} (いざなみのみこと) '''{{jalink|Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami}} File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Tsushima-jinja.jpg|Tsushima Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner1.jpg|Combined shrine hall (Hakusan Shrine, Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine) File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Akiba-jinja.jpg|Akiba Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner2.jpg|Subsidiary shrines === Festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival {{jalink|January 1|Q2150}} * Hatsu-uma Festival (early {{jalink|February|Q109}}) * Tsushima Shrine {{jalink|annual festival|Q11385469}} ({{jalink|July|Q121}}, Marine Day) * Kaze Festival {{jalink|September 1|Q2859}} * Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine Annual Grand Festival ** Eve Festival: first Saturday of {{jalink|October|Q124}} ** Grand Festival: the following Sunday * Shichi-Go-San celebration (mid {{jalink|November|Q125}}) * New Year’s Eve Festival (late night of {{jalink|December 31|Q2912}}) === On-site information === ;Address * 847 {{jalink|Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|Ōmaru}}, {{jalink|Inagi City|Q317847}}, {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490}} ;Access * Nearest station: About an 8-minute walk (approx. 600 m south) from {{jalink|Minami-Tama Station|Q5366901}} on the JR {{jalink|Nambu Line|Q1366263}} ;Surroundings Located on the northern edge of the central {{jalink|Tama Hills|Q1502721}}, the shrine offers a panoramic view from its precincts of the {{jalink|Tama River lowland|Q131910796|alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River}}. The wooded hill behind the precincts was formerly called Shiro-yama; in the medieval period, a mountain castle known as Ōmaru Castle, a {{jalink|yamajiro|Q15710038}}, was built there. Today, the area forms part of the {{jalink|Tama New Town|Q7680510}} {{jalink|Kōyōdai (Inagi City)|Q11416047|Kōyōdai}} residential district. Along both sides of the approach to the shrine are the Buddhist temples Enshō-ji and Jikishin-an, and to the north of the precincts is another Buddhist temple, Fumon Zen’an. === Notes === === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Further reading === * {{cite book|language=ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Vol. 95, Tama District no. 7|chapter=Ōmaru Village, Marumiya Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/50}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884–6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Omatonotsunotenjinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:稲城市の建築物]] [[Category:稲城市の歴史]] [[Category:qq]]

Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine''' (おおまとのつのてんじんしゃ; also read おおまとのつのあまつかみのやしろ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|Ōmaru}}, {{jalink|Inagi City|Q317847}}, {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490}}. Its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main sanctuary * {{jalink|Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939}} (くしまちのみこと) : A deity said to be the father of Ame-no-Sokotachi no MikotoJunzaburō Hayakawa, “Watarai-shi Keizu,” *Keizu Sōran*, vol. 2, Kokushokankōkai, 1915, p. 396.. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the entry for the Ame-no-Kagu-yama-ni-masu Kushimachi no Mikoto Shrine in {{jalink|Tōichi District|Q11405036}}, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} (present-day {{jalink|Amekaguyama Shrine|Q98082987}}, located on the northern foothills of {{jalink|Mt. Amekaguyama|Q11443780}} in {{jalink|Kashihara City|Q496319}}, Nara Prefecture) includes the note “former name: Ōmatonochi no Kami.” From this, it has been considered that this shrine enshrines the same deity as Amekaguyama Shrine. Until the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the shrine was called Marumiya Myōjin, which is said to be a corruption of “Matono-no-miya Myōjin”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}. === History === [[Image:Torii of Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Torii gate (May 2009)]] [[Image:Komainu in Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Komainu. Inscribed “An auspicious day in the sixth month of the twelfth year of {{jalink|Tenpō|Q1369045}}, {{jalink|Kanoto-ushi|Q904956}}” ({{jalink|1841|Q7622}}). (May 2009)]] [[Image:View from Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the precincts over the {{jalink|alluvial plain}} of the middle reaches of the {{jalink|Tama River|Q1063006}}. {{jalink|Inagi Bridge|Q11596545}} can be seen spanning the Tama River. (May 2009)]] Although it is a very small shrine and its origins are unclear, the *{{jalink|Report on Shikinaisha Investigations}}* identifies this shrine as the “Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine” listed in the *{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}* for {{jalink|Tama District|Q11430789}}, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}. However, regarding the Shikinaisha Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine, {{jalink|Musashi Mitake Shrine|Q6587276}} in {{jalink|Ōme City|Q237683}}, Tokyo, is also considered a {{jalink|theoretical identification|Q135022904}}, and in the early {{jalink|Meiji era|Q3125096}} Musashi Mitake Shrine was known as Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine. The name “Ōmatono-tsu” is thought to mean “a landing place on a large, round, flat plain”Inagi City Board of Education (ed.), *Inagi no Chimei to Kyūdō*, p. 73.. In fact, the former place name of the lowland in front of {{jalink|Fumon Zen’an}} adjacent to the shrine precincts was “Irigata,” and it was a {{jalink|lagoon}} of the {{jalink|Old Tama River}} sandwiched between Tenjin-yama and Shiro-yama (which contains remains of a {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}} fort). The place name Ōmaru in Inagi City, where this shrine stands, is also said to mean “large, round, flat plain.” This area has been one of the river-crossing points of the {{jalink|Tama River|Q1063006}} since ancient times (where the {{jalink|Fuchū Highway|Q11486060}} and the Kawasaki Highway converge), so it is reasonable to think that there was a {{jalink|river port|Q11555884}} here. Incidentally, the aforementioned Shiro-yama is also thought to have been built to control the Tama River crossing. === Subsidiary shrines === '''{{jalink|Akiba Shrine|Q11595841}}'''{{efn|The section on Marumiya Shrine in the *{{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|Q11503943}}* includes the note “subsidiary shrine: Akiba Shrine”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}.}} :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Hi-no-Kagutsuchi Ōkami}} '''{{jalink|Tsushima Shrine|Q705136}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Takesusanoo no Mikoto}} :[Event] Annual grand festival (Marine Day in July) '''{{jalink|Inari Shrine|Q719665}}''' × 2 :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} :[Event] Hatsu-uma Festival (early February) '''{{jalink|Hakusan Shrine|Q11579557}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Kikuri-hime no Mikoto}} (くくりひめのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Izanagi no Mikoto}} (いざなぎのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Izanami no Mikoto}} (いざなみのみこと) '''{{jalink|Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami}} File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Tsushima-jinja.jpg|Tsushima Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner1.jpg|Combined shrine hall (Hakusan Shrine, Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine) File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Akiba-jinja.jpg|Akiba Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner2.jpg|Subsidiary shrines === Festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival {{jalink|January 1|Q2150}} * Hatsu-uma Festival (early {{jalink|February|Q109}}) * Tsushima Shrine {{jalink|annual festival|Q11385469}} ({{jalink|July|Q121}}, Marine Day) * Kaze Festival {{jalink|September 1|Q2859}} * Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine Annual Grand Festival ** Eve Festival: first Saturday of {{jalink|October|Q124}} ** Grand Festival: the following Sunday * Shichi-Go-San celebration (mid {{jalink|November|Q125}}) * New Year’s Eve Festival (late night of {{jalink|December 31|Q2912}}) === On-site information === ;Address * 847 {{jalink|Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|Ōmaru}}, {{jalink|Inagi City|Q317847}}, {{jalink|Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490}} ;Access * Nearest station: About an 8-minute walk (approx. 600 m south) from {{jalink|Minami-Tama Station|Q5366901}} on the JR {{jalink|Nambu Line|Q1366263}} ;Surroundings Located on the northern edge of the central {{jalink|Tama Hills|Q1502721}}, the shrine offers a panoramic view from its precincts of the {{jalink|Tama River lowland|Q131910796|alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River}}. The wooded hill behind the precincts was formerly called Shiro-yama; in the medieval period, a mountain castle known as Ōmaru Castle, a {{jalink|yamajiro|Q15710038}}, was built there. Today, the area forms part of the {{jalink|Tama New Town|Q7680510}} {{jalink|Kōyōdai (Inagi City)|Q11416047|Kōyōdai}} residential district. Along both sides of the approach to the shrine are the Buddhist temples Enshō-ji and Jikishin-an, and to the north of the precincts is another Buddhist temple, Fumon Zen’an. === Notes === === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Further reading === * {{cite book|language=ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Vol. 95, Tama District no. 7|chapter=Ōmaru Village, Marumiya Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/50}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884–6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Omatonotsunotenjinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:稲城市の建築物]] [[Category:稲城市の歴史]]

Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine Annual Grand Festival ** Eve Festival: first Saturday of {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124}} ** Grand Festival: the following Sunday * Shichi-Go-San celebration (mid {{ill|lt=November|Q125|WD=Q125}}) * New Year’s Eve Festival (late night of {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) === On-site information === ;Address * 847 {{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru}}, {{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490}} ;Access * Nearest station: About an 8-minute walk (approx. 600 m south) from {{ill|lt=Minami-Tama Station|Q5366901|WD=Q5366901}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Nambu Line|Q1366263|WD=Q1366263}} ;Surroundings Located on the northern edge of the central {{ill|lt=Tama Hills|Q1502721|WD=Q1502721}}, the shrine offers a panoramic view from its precincts of the {{ill|lt=Tama River lowland|Q131910796|WD=Q131910796|lt=alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River}}. The wooded hill behind the precincts was formerly called Shiro-yama; in the medieval period, a mountain castle known as Ōmaru Castle, a {{ill|lt=yamajiro|Q15710038|WD=Q15710038}}, was built there. Today, the area forms part of the {{ill|lt=Tama New Town|Q7680510|WD=Q7680510}} {{ill|lt=Kōyōdai (Inagi City)|Q11416047|WD=Q11416047|lt=Kōyōdai}} residential district. Along both sides of the approach to the shrine are the Buddhist temples Enshō-ji and Jikishin-an, and to the north of the precincts is another Buddhist temple, Fumon Zen’an. === Notes === === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Further reading === * {{cite book|language=ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Vol. 95, Tama District no. 7|chapter=Ōmaru Village, Marumiya Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/50}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884–6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Omatonotsunotenjinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:稲城市の建築物]] [[Category:稲城市の歴史]]

(Inagi)}} [[JA:大麻止乃豆乃天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11442032}} == Japanese content == {{Original research|date=December 28, 2016 (Wed) 05:25 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine |Image = [[File:Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (May 2009) |Location = {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}{{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847}}{{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru}} 847 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939|WD=Q108039939}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}} (theoretical identification); former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Established = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Other name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = The Sunday following the first Saturday of {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Ōmatono
Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine''' (おおまとのつのてんじんしゃ; also read おおまとのつのあまつかみのやしろ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru}}, {{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main sanctuary * {{ill|lt=Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939|WD=Q108039939}} (くしまちのみこと) : A deity said to be the father of Ame-no-Sokotachi no MikotoJunzaburō Hayakawa, “Watarai-shi Keizu,” *Keizu Sōran*, vol. 2, Kokushokankōkai, 1915, p. 396.. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the entry for the Ame-no-Kagu-yama-ni-masu Kushimachi no Mikoto Shrine in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Amekaguyama Shrine|Q98082987|WD=Q98082987}}, located on the northern foothills of {{ill|lt=Mt. Amekaguyama|Q11443780|WD=Q11443780}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319}}, Nara Prefecture) includes the note “former name: Ōmatonochi no Kami.” From this, it has been considered that this shrine enshrines the same deity as Amekaguyama Shrine. Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, the shrine was called Marumiya Myōjin, which is said to be a corruption of “Matono-no-miya Myōjin”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}. === History === [[Image:Torii of Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Torii gate (May 2009)]] [[Image:Komainu in Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Komainu. Inscribed “An auspicious day in the sixth month of the twelfth year of {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}}, {{ill|lt=Kanoto-ushi|Q904956|WD=Q904956}}” ({{ill|lt=1841|Q7622|WD=Q7622}}). (May 2009)]] [[Image:View from Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the precincts over the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=alluvial plain|UNKNOWN}} of the middle reaches of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006}}. {{ill|lt=Inagi Bridge|Q11596545|WD=Q11596545}} can be seen spanning the Tama River. (May 2009)]] Although it is a very small shrine and its origins are unclear, the *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Report on Shikinaisha Investigations|UNKNOWN}}* identifies this shrine as the “Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine” listed in the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}* for {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}. However, regarding the Shikinaisha Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine, {{ill|lt=Musashi Mitake Shrine|Q6587276|WD=Q6587276}} in {{ill|lt=Ōme City|Q237683|WD=Q237683}}, Tokyo, is also considered a {{ill|lt=theoretical identification|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}, and in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} Musashi Mitake Shrine was known as Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine. The name “Ōmatono-tsu” is thought to mean “a landing place on a large, round, flat plain”Inagi City Board of Education (ed.), *Inagi no Chimei to Kyūdō*, p. 73.. In fact, the former place name of the lowland in front of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fumon Zen’an|UNKNOWN}} adjacent to the shrine precincts was “Irigata,” and it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lagoon|UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Old Tama River|UNKNOWN}} sandwiched between Tenjin-yama and Shiro-yama (which contains remains of a {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} fort). The place name Ōmaru in Inagi City, where this shrine stands, is also said to mean “large, round, flat plain.” This area has been one of the river-crossing points of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006}} since ancient times (where the {{ill|lt=Fuchū Highway|Q11486060|WD=Q11486060}} and the Kawasaki Highway converge), so it is reasonable to think that there was a {{ill|lt=river port|Q11555884|WD=Q11555884}} here. Incidentally, the aforementioned Shiro-yama is also thought to have been built to control the Tama River crossing. === Subsidiary shrines === '''{{ill|lt=Akiba Shrine|Q11595841|WD=Q11595841}}'''{{efn|The section on Marumiya Shrine in the *{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}* includes the note “subsidiary shrine: Akiba Shrine”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}.}} :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi Ōkami}} '''{{ill|lt=Tsushima Shrine|Q705136|WD=Q705136}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} :[Event] Annual grand festival (Marine Day in July) '''{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q719665|WD=Q719665}}''' × 2 :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} :[Event] Hatsu-uma Festival (early February) '''{{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikuri-hime no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} (くくりひめのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} (いざなぎのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} (いざなみのみこと) '''{{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|UNKNOWN}} File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Tsushima-jinja.jpg|Tsushima Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner1.jpg|Combined shrine hall (Hakusan Shrine, Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine) File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Akiba-jinja.jpg|Akiba Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner2.jpg|Subsidiary shrines === Festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}} * Hatsu-uma Festival (early {{ill|lt=February|Q109|WD=Q109}}) * Tsushima Shrine {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} ({{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121}}, Marine Day) * Kaze Festival {{ill|lt=September 1|Q2859|WD=Q2859}} * Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine Annual Grand Festival ** Eve Festival: first Saturday of {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124}} ** Grand Festival: the following Sunday * Shichi-Go-San celebration (mid {{ill|lt=November|Q125|WD=Q125}}) * New Year’s Eve Festival (late night of {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) === On-site information === ;Address * 847 {{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru}}, {{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490}} ;Access * Nearest station: About an 8-minute walk (approx. 600 m south) from {{ill|lt=Minami-Tama Station|Q5366901|WD=Q5366901}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Nambu Line|Q1366263|WD=Q1366263}} ;Surroundings Located on the northern edge of the central {{ill|lt=Tama Hills|Q1502721|WD=Q1502721}}, the shrine offers a panoramic view from its precincts of the {{ill|lt=Tama River lowland|Q131910796|WD=Q131910796|lt=alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River}}. The wooded hill behind the precincts was formerly called Shiro-yama; in the medieval period, a mountain castle known as Ōmaru Castle, a {{ill|lt=yamajiro|Q15710038|WD=Q15710038}}, was built there. Today, the area forms part of the {{ill|lt=Tama New Town|Q7680510|WD=Q7680510}} {{ill|lt=Kōyōdai (Inagi City)|Q11416047|WD=Q11416047|lt=Kōyōdai}} residential district. Along both sides of the approach to the shrine are the Buddhist temples Enshō-ji and Jikishin-an, and to the north of the precincts is another Buddhist temple, Fumon Zen’an. === Notes === === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Further reading === * {{cite book|language=ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Vol. 95, Tama District no. 7|chapter=Ōmaru Village, Marumiya Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/50}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884–6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Omatonotsunotenjinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:稲城市の建築物]] [[Category:稲城市の歴史]]

(Inagi)}} [[JA:大麻止乃豆乃天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11442032}} == Japanese content == {{Original research|date=December 28, 2016 (Wed) 05:25 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine |Image = [[File:Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (May 2009) |Location = {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}{{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847}}{{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru}} 847 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939|WD=Q108039939}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}} (theoretical identification); former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Established = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Other name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = The Sunday following the first Saturday of {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Ōmatono
Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine''' (おおまとのつのてんじんしゃ; also read おおまとのつのあまつかみのやしろ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru}}, {{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main sanctuary * {{ill|lt=Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939|WD=Q108039939}} (くしまちのみこと) : A deity said to be the father of Ame-no-Sokotachi no MikotoJunzaburō Hayakawa, “Watarai-shi Keizu,” *Keizu Sōran*, vol. 2, Kokushokankōkai, 1915, p. 396.. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the entry for the Ame-no-Kagu-yama-ni-masu Kushimachi no Mikoto Shrine in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Amekaguyama Shrine|Q98082987|WD=Q98082987}}, located on the northern foothills of {{ill|lt=Mt. Amekaguyama|Q11443780|WD=Q11443780}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319}}, Nara Prefecture) includes the note “former name: Ōmatonochi no Kami.” From this, it has been considered that this shrine enshrines the same deity as Amekaguyama Shrine. Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, the shrine was called Marumiya Myōjin, which is said to be a corruption of “Matono-no-miya Myōjin”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}. === History === [[Image:Torii of Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Torii gate (May 2009)]] [[Image:Komainu in Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Komainu. Inscribed “An auspicious day in the sixth month of the twelfth year of {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}}, {{ill|lt=Kanoto-ushi|Q904956|WD=Q904956}}” ({{ill|lt=1841|Q7622|WD=Q7622}}). (May 2009)]] [[Image:View from Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the precincts over the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=alluvial plain|1=UNKNOWN}} of the middle reaches of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006}}. {{ill|lt=Inagi Bridge|Q11596545|WD=Q11596545}} can be seen spanning the Tama River. (May 2009)]] Although it is a very small shrine and its origins are unclear, the *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Report on Shikinaisha Investigations|1=UNKNOWN}}* identifies this shrine as the “Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine” listed in the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}* for {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}. However, regarding the Shikinaisha Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine, {{ill|lt=Musashi Mitake Shrine|Q6587276|WD=Q6587276}} in {{ill|lt=Ōme City|Q237683|WD=Q237683}}, Tokyo, is also considered a {{ill|lt=theoretical identification|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}, and in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} Musashi Mitake Shrine was known as Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine. The name “Ōmatono-tsu” is thought to mean “a landing place on a large, round, flat plain”Inagi City Board of Education (ed.), *Inagi no Chimei to Kyūdō*, p. 73.. In fact, the former place name of the lowland in front of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fumon Zen’an|1=UNKNOWN}} adjacent to the shrine precincts was “Irigata,” and it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lagoon|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Old Tama River|1=UNKNOWN}} sandwiched between Tenjin-yama and Shiro-yama (which contains remains of a {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} fort). The place name Ōmaru in Inagi City, where this shrine stands, is also said to mean “large, round, flat plain.” This area has been one of the river-crossing points of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006}} since ancient times (where the {{ill|lt=Fuchū Highway|Q11486060|WD=Q11486060}} and the Kawasaki Highway converge), so it is reasonable to think that there was a {{ill|lt=river port|Q11555884|WD=Q11555884}} here. Incidentally, the aforementioned Shiro-yama is also thought to have been built to control the Tama River crossing. === Subsidiary shrines === '''{{ill|lt=Akiba Shrine|Q11595841|WD=Q11595841}}'''{{efn|The section on Marumiya Shrine in the *{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}* includes the note “subsidiary shrine: Akiba Shrine”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}.}} :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi Ōkami}} '''{{ill|lt=Tsushima Shrine|Q705136|WD=Q705136}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} :[Event] Annual grand festival (Marine Day in July) '''{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q719665|WD=Q719665}}''' × 2 :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} :[Event] Hatsu-uma Festival (early February) '''{{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikuri-hime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (くくりひめのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (いざなぎのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (いざなみのみこと) '''{{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Tsushima-jinja.jpg|Tsushima Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner1.jpg|Combined shrine hall (Hakusan Shrine, Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine) File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Akiba-jinja.jpg|Akiba Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner2.jpg|Subsidiary shrines === Festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}} * Hatsu-uma Festival (early {{ill|lt=February|Q109|WD=Q109}}) * Tsushima Shrine {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} ({{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121}}, Marine Day) * Kaze Festival {{ill|lt=September 1|Q2859|WD=Q2859}} * Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine Annual Grand Festival ** Eve Festival: first Saturday of {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124}} ** Grand Festival: the following Sunday * Shichi-Go-San celebration (mid {{ill|lt=November|Q125|WD=Q125}}) * New Year’s Eve Festival (late night of {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) === On-site information === ;Address * 847 {{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru}}, {{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490}} ;Access * Nearest station: About an 8-minute walk (approx. 600 m south) from {{ill|lt=Minami-Tama Station|Q5366901|WD=Q5366901}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Nambu Line|Q1366263|WD=Q1366263}} ;Surroundings Located on the northern edge of the central {{ill|lt=Tama Hills|Q1502721|WD=Q1502721}}, the shrine offers a panoramic view from its precincts of the {{ill|lt=Tama River lowland|Q131910796|WD=Q131910796|lt=alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River}}. The wooded hill behind the precincts was formerly called Shiro-yama; in the medieval period, a mountain castle known as Ōmaru Castle, a {{ill|lt=yamajiro|Q15710038|WD=Q15710038}}, was built there. Today, the area forms part of the {{ill|lt=Tama New Town|Q7680510|WD=Q7680510}} {{ill|lt=Kōyōdai (Inagi City)|Q11416047|WD=Q11416047|lt=Kōyōdai}} residential district. Along both sides of the approach to the shrine are the Buddhist temples Enshō-ji and Jikishin-an, and to the north of the precincts is another Buddhist temple, Fumon Zen’an. === Notes === === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Further reading === * {{cite book|language=ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Vol. 95, Tama District no. 7|chapter=Ōmaru Village, Marumiya Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/50}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884–6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Omatonotsunotenjinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:稲城市の建築物]] [[Category:稲城市の歴史]]
