Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main sanctuary * {{ill|lt=Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939|WD=Q108039939}} (くしまちのみこと) : A deity said to be the father of Ame-no-Sokotachi no MikotoJunzaburō Hayakawa, “Watarai-shi Keizu,” *Keizu Sōran*, vol. 2, Kokushokankōkai, 1915, p. 396.. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the entry for the Ame-no-Kagu-yama-ni-masu Kushimachi no Mikoto Shrine in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Amekaguyama Shrine|Q98082987|WD=Q98082987}}, located on the northern foothills of {{ill|lt=Mt. Amekaguyama|Q11443780|WD=Q11443780}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319}}, Nara Prefecture) includes the note “former name: Ōmatonochi no Kami.” From this, it has been considered that this shrine enshrines the same deity as Amekaguyama Shrine. Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, the shrine was called Marumiya Myōjin, which is said to be a corruption of “Matono-no-miya Myōjin”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}. === History === [[Image:Torii of Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Torii gate (May 2009)]] [[Image:Komainu in Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Komainu. Inscribed “An auspicious day in the sixth month of the twelfth year of {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}}, {{ill|lt=Kanoto-ushi|Q904956|WD=Q904956}}” ({{ill|lt=1841|Q7622|WD=Q7622}}). (May 2009)]] [[Image:View from Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the precincts over the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=alluvial plain|1=UNKNOWN}} of the middle reaches of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006}}. {{ill|lt=Inagi Bridge|Q11596545|WD=Q11596545}} can be seen spanning the Tama River. (May 2009)]] Although it is a very small shrine and its origins are unclear, the *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Report on Shikinaisha Investigations|1=UNKNOWN}}* identifies this shrine as the “Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine” listed in the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}* for {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}. However, regarding the Shikinaisha Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine, {{ill|lt=Musashi Mitake Shrine|Q6587276|WD=Q6587276}} in {{ill|lt=Ōme City|Q237683|WD=Q237683}}, Tokyo, is also considered a {{ill|lt=theoretical identification|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}, and in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} Musashi Mitake Shrine was known as Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine. The name “Ōmatono-tsu” is thought to mean “a landing place on a large, round, flat plain”Inagi City Board of Education (ed.), *Inagi no Chimei to Kyūdō*, p. 73.. In fact, the former place name of the lowland in front of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fumon Zen’an|1=UNKNOWN}} adjacent to the shrine precincts was “Irigata,” and it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lagoon|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Old Tama River|1=UNKNOWN}} sandwiched between Tenjin-yama and Shiro-yama (which contains remains of a {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} fort). The place name Ōmaru in Inagi City, where this shrine stands, is also said to mean “large, round, flat plain.” This area has been one of the river-crossing points of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006}} since ancient times (where the {{ill|lt=Fuchū Highway|Q11486060|WD=Q11486060}} and the Kawasaki Highway converge), so it is reasonable to think that there was a {{ill|lt=river port|Q11555884|WD=Q11555884}} here. Incidentally, the aforementioned Shiro-yama is also thought to have been built to control the Tama River crossing. === Subsidiary shrines === '''{{ill|lt=Akiba Shrine|Q11595841|WD=Q11595841}}'''{{efn|The section on Marumiya Shrine in the *{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}* includes the note “subsidiary shrine: Akiba Shrine”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}.}} :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi Ōkami}} '''{{ill|lt=Tsushima Shrine|Q705136|WD=Q705136}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} :[Event] Annual grand festival (Marine Day in July) '''{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q719665|WD=Q719665}}''' × 2 :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} :[Event] Hatsu-uma Festival (early February) '''{{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikuri-hime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (くくりひめのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (いざなぎのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (いざなみのみこと) '''{{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q455602}} File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Tsushima-jinja.jpg|Tsushima Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner1.jpg|Combined shrine hall (Hakusan Shrine, Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine) File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Akiba-jinja.jpg|Akiba Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner2.jpg|Subsidiary shrines === Festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}} * Hatsu-uma Festival (early {{ill|lt=February|Q109|WD=Q109}}) * Tsushima Shrine {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} ({{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121}}, Marine Day) * Kaze Festival {{ill|lt=September 1|Q2859|WD=Q2859}} * Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine Annual Grand Festival ** Eve Festival: first Saturday of {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124}} ** Grand Festival: the following Sunday * Shichi-Go-San celebration (mid {{ill|lt=November|Q125|WD=Q125}}) * New Year’s Eve Festival (late night of {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) === On-site information === ;Address * 847 {{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru}}, {{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490}} ;Access * Nearest station: About an 8-minute walk (approx. 600 m south) from {{ill|lt=Minami-Tama Station|Q5366901|WD=Q5366901}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Nambu Line|Q1366263|WD=Q1366263}} ;Surroundings Located on the northern edge of the central {{ill|lt=Tama Hills|Q1502721|WD=Q1502721}}, the shrine offers a panoramic view from its precincts of the {{ill|lt=Tama River lowland|Q131910796|WD=Q131910796|lt=alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River}}. The wooded hill behind the precincts was formerly called Shiro-yama; in the medieval period, a mountain castle known as Ōmaru Castle, a {{ill|lt=yamajiro|Q15710038|WD=Q15710038}}, was built there. Today, the area forms part of the {{ill|lt=Tama New Town|Q7680510|WD=Q7680510}} {{ill|lt=Kōyōdai (Inagi City)|Q11416047|WD=Q11416047|lt=Kōyōdai}} residential district. Along both sides of the approach to the shrine are the Buddhist temples Enshō-ji and Jikishin-an, and to the north of the precincts is another Buddhist temple, Fumon Zen’an. === Notes === === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Further reading === * {{cite book|language=ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Vol. 95, Tama District no. 7|chapter=Ōmaru Village, Marumiya Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/50}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884–6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Omatonotsunotenjinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:稲城市の建築物]] [[Category:稲城市の歴史]]

{{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124}} ** Grand Festival: the following Sunday * Shichi-Go-San celebration (mid {{ill|lt=November|Q125|WD=Q125}}) * New Year’s Eve Festival (late night of {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) === On-site information === ;Address * 847 {{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru}}, {{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490}} ;Access * Nearest station: About an 8-minute walk (approx. 600 m south) from {{ill|lt=Minami-Tama Station|Q5366901|WD=Q5366901}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Nambu Line|Q1366263|WD=Q1366263}} ;Surroundings Located on the northern edge of the central {{ill|lt=Tama Hills|Q1502721|WD=Q1502721}}, the shrine offers a panoramic view from its precincts of the {{ill|lt=Tama River lowland|Q131910796|WD=Q131910796|lt=alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River}}. The wooded hill behind the precincts was formerly called Shiro-yama; in the medieval period, a mountain castle known as Ōmaru Castle, a {{ill|lt=yamajiro|Q15710038|WD=Q15710038}}, was built there. Today, the area forms part of the {{ill|lt=Tama New Town|Q7680510|WD=Q7680510}} {{ill|lt=Kōyōdai (Inagi City)|Q11416047|WD=Q11416047|lt=Kōyōdai}} residential district. Along both sides of the approach to the shrine are the Buddhist temples Enshō-ji and Jikishin-an, and to the north of the precincts is another Buddhist temple, Fumon Zen’an. === Notes === === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Further reading === * {{cite book|language=ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Vol. 95, Tama District no. 7|chapter=Ōmaru Village, Marumiya Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/50}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884–6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Omatonotsunotenjinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:稲城市の建築物]] [[Category:稲城市の歴史]]

(Inagi)}} [[JA:大麻止乃豆乃天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11442032}} == Japanese content == {{Original research|date=December 28, 2016 (Wed) 05:25 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine |Image = [[File:Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (May 2009) |Location = {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}{{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847}}{{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru}} 847 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939|WD=Q108039939}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}} (theoretical identification); former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Established = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Other name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = The Sunday following the first Saturday of {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Ōmatono
Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine''' (おおまとのつのてんじんしゃ; also read おおまとのつのあまつかみのやしろ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru}}, {{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main sanctuary * {{ill|lt=Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939|WD=Q108039939}} (くしまちのみこと) : A deity said to be the father of Ame-no-Sokotachi no MikotoJunzaburō Hayakawa, “Watarai-shi Keizu,” *Keizu Sōran*, vol. 2, Kokushokankōkai, 1915, p. 396.. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the entry for the Ame-no-Kagu-yama-ni-masu Kushimachi no Mikoto Shrine in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Amekaguyama Shrine|Q98082987|WD=Q98082987}}, located on the northern foothills of {{ill|lt=Mt. Amekaguyama|Q11443780|WD=Q11443780}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319}}, Nara Prefecture) includes the note “former name: Ōmatonochi no Kami.” From this, it has been considered that this shrine enshrines the same deity as Amekaguyama Shrine. Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, the shrine was called Marumiya Myōjin, which is said to be a corruption of “Matono-no-miya Myōjin”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}. === History === [[Image:Torii of Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Torii gate (May 2009)]] [[Image:Komainu in Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Komainu. Inscribed “An auspicious day in the sixth month of the twelfth year of {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}}, {{ill|lt=Kanoto-ushi|Q904956|WD=Q904956}}” ({{ill|lt=1841|Q7622|WD=Q7622}}). (May 2009)]] [[Image:View from Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the precincts over the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=alluvial plain|1=UNKNOWN}} of the middle reaches of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006}}. {{ill|lt=Inagi Bridge|Q11596545|WD=Q11596545}} can be seen spanning the Tama River. (May 2009)]] Although it is a very small shrine and its origins are unclear, the *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Report on Shikinaisha Investigations|1=UNKNOWN}}* identifies this shrine as the “Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine” listed in the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}* for {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}. However, regarding the Shikinaisha Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine, {{ill|lt=Musashi Mitake Shrine|Q6587276|WD=Q6587276}} in {{ill|lt=Ōme City|Q237683|WD=Q237683}}, Tokyo, is also considered a {{ill|lt=theoretical identification|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}, and in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} Musashi Mitake Shrine was known as Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine. The name “Ōmatono-tsu” is thought to mean “a landing place on a large, round, flat plain”Inagi City Board of Education (ed.), *Inagi no Chimei to Kyūdō*, p. 73.. In fact, the former place name of the lowland in front of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fumon Zen’an|1=UNKNOWN}} adjacent to the shrine precincts was “Irigata,” and it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lagoon|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Old Tama River|1=UNKNOWN}} sandwiched between Tenjin-yama and Shiro-yama (which contains remains of a {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} fort). The place name Ōmaru in Inagi City, where this shrine stands, is also said to mean “large, round, flat plain.” This area has been one of the river-crossing points of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006}} since ancient times (where the {{ill|lt=Fuchū Highway|Q11486060|WD=Q11486060}} and the Kawasaki Highway converge), so it is reasonable to think that there was a {{ill|lt=river port|Q11555884|WD=Q11555884}} here. Incidentally, the aforementioned Shiro-yama is also thought to have been built to control the Tama River crossing. === Subsidiary shrines === '''{{ill|lt=Akiba Shrine|Q11595841|WD=Q11595841}}'''{{efn|The section on Marumiya Shrine in the *{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}* includes the note “subsidiary shrine: Akiba Shrine”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}.}} :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi Ōkami}} '''{{ill|lt=Tsushima Shrine|Q705136|WD=Q705136}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} :[Event] Annual grand festival (Marine Day in July) '''{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q719665|WD=Q719665}}''' × 2 :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} :[Event] Hatsu-uma Festival (early February) '''{{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikuri-hime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (くくりひめのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (いざなぎのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (いざなみのみこと) '''{{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q455602}} File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Tsushima-jinja.jpg|Tsushima Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner1.jpg|Combined shrine hall (Hakusan Shrine, Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine) File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Akiba-jinja.jpg|Akiba Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner2.jpg|Subsidiary shrines === Festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}} * Hatsu-uma Festival (early {{ill|lt=February|Q109|WD=Q109}}) * Tsushima Shrine {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}} ({{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121}}, Marine Day) * Kaze Festival {{ill|lt=September 1|Q2859|WD=Q2859}} * Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine Annual Grand Festival ** Eve Festival: first Saturday of {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124}} ** Grand Festival: the following Sunday * Shichi-Go-San celebration (mid {{ill|lt=November|Q125|WD=Q125}}) * New Year’s Eve Festival (late night of {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) === On-site information === ;Address * 847 {{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru}}, {{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490}} ;Access * Nearest station: About an 8-minute walk (approx. 600 m south) from {{ill|lt=Minami-Tama Station|Q5366901|WD=Q5366901}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Nambu Line|Q1366263|WD=Q1366263}} ;Surroundings Located on the northern edge of the central {{ill|lt=Tama Hills|Q1502721|WD=Q1502721}}, the shrine offers a panoramic view from its precincts of the {{ill|lt=Tama River lowland|Q131910796|WD=Q131910796|lt=alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River}}. The wooded hill behind the precincts was formerly called Shiro-yama; in the medieval period, a mountain castle known as Ōmaru Castle, a {{ill|lt=yamajiro|Q15710038|WD=Q15710038}}, was built there. Today, the area forms part of the {{ill|lt=Tama New Town|Q7680510|WD=Q7680510}} {{ill|lt=Kōyōdai (Inagi City)|Q11416047|WD=Q11416047|lt=Kōyōdai}} residential district. Along both sides of the approach to the shrine are the Buddhist temples Enshō-ji and Jikishin-an, and to the north of the precincts is another Buddhist temple, Fumon Zen’an. === Notes === === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Further reading === * {{cite book|language=ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Vol. 95, Tama District no. 7|chapter=Ōmaru Village, Marumiya Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/50}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884–6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Omatonotsunotenjinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:稲城市の建築物]] [[Category:稲城市の歴史]]

== {{Original research|date=December 28, 2016 (Wed) 05:25 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine |Image = [[File:Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (May 2009) |Location = {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847|1=Inagi}}{{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru|1=Ōmaru}} 847 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939|WD=Q108039939|1=Kushimachi no Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} (theoretical identification); former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = The Sunday following the first Saturday of {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124|1=October}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Ōmatono
Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine''' (おおまとのつのてんじんしゃ; also read おおまとのつのあまつかみのやしろ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru|1=Ōmaru}}, {{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847|1=Inagi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main sanctuary * {{ill|lt=Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939|WD=Q108039939|1=Kushimachi no Mikoto}} (くしまちのみこと) : A deity said to be the father of Ame-no-Sokotachi no MikotoJunzaburō Hayakawa, “Watarai-shi Keizu,” *Keizu Sōran*, vol. 2, Kokushokankōkai, 1915, p. 396.. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the entry for the Ame-no-Kagu-yama-ni-masu Kushimachi no Mikoto Shrine in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Amekaguyama Shrine|Q98082987|WD=Q98082987|1=Amanokaguyama Shrine}}, located on the northern foothills of {{ill|lt=Mt. Amekaguyama|Q11443780|WD=Q11443780|1=Mount Amanokagu}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture) includes the note “former name: Ōmatonochi no Kami.” From this, it has been considered that this shrine enshrines the same deity as Amekaguyama Shrine. Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was called Marumiya Myōjin, which is said to be a corruption of “Matono-no-miya Myōjin”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}. === History === [[Image:Torii of Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Torii gate (May 2009)]] [[Image:Komainu in Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Komainu. Inscribed “An auspicious day in the sixth month of the twelfth year of {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}}, {{ill|lt=Kanoto-ushi|Q904956|WD=Q904956|1=Yin Metal Ox}}” ({{ill|lt=1841|Q7622|WD=Q7622|1=1841}}). (May 2009)]] [[Image:View from Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the precincts over the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=alluvial plain|1=UNKNOWN}} of the middle reaches of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}}. {{ill|lt=Inagi Bridge|Q11596545|WD=Q11596545|1=Inagi Bridge}} can be seen spanning the Tama River. (May 2009)]] Although it is a very small shrine and its origins are unclear, the *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Report on Shikinaisha Investigations|1=UNKNOWN}}* identifies this shrine as the “Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine” listed in the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}* for {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789|1=Tama district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}. However, regarding the Shikinaisha Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine, {{ill|lt=Musashi Mitake Shrine|Q6587276|WD=Q6587276|1=Musashi-Mitake Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōme City|Q237683|WD=Q237683|1=Ōme, Tokyo}}, Tokyo, is also considered a {{ill|lt=theoretical identification|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} Musashi Mitake Shrine was known as Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine. The name “Ōmatono-tsu” is thought to mean “a landing place on a large, round, flat plain”Inagi City Board of Education (ed.), *Inagi no Chimei to Kyūdō*, p. 73.. In fact, the former place name of the lowland in front of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fumon Zen’an|1=UNKNOWN}} adjacent to the shrine precincts was “Irigata,” and it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lagoon|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Old Tama River|1=UNKNOWN}} sandwiched between Tenjin-yama and Shiro-yama (which contains remains of a {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} fort). The place name Ōmaru in Inagi City, where this shrine stands, is also said to mean “large, round, flat plain.” This area has been one of the river-crossing points of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}} since ancient times (where the {{ill|lt=Fuchū Highway|Q11486060|WD=Q11486060|1=Fuchū Highway}} and the Kawasaki Highway converge), so it is reasonable to think that there was a {{ill|lt=river port|Q11555884|WD=Q11555884|1=river port}} here. Incidentally, the aforementioned Shiro-yama is also thought to have been built to control the Tama River crossing. === Subsidiary shrines === '''{{ill|lt=Akiba Shrine|Q11595841|WD=Q11595841|1=Akiba Shrines}}'''{{efn|The section on Marumiya Shrine in the *{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}* includes the note “subsidiary shrine: Akiba Shrine”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}.}} :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} '''{{ill|lt=Tsushima Shrine|Q705136|WD=Q705136|1=Tsushima Shrine}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} :[Event] Annual grand festival (Marine Day in July) '''{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}''' × 2 :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} :[Event] Hatsu-uma Festival (early February) '''{{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikuri-hime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (くくりひめのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (いざなぎのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (いざなみのみこと) '''{{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|1=Shinmei shrines}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Tsushima-jinja.jpg|Tsushima Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner1.jpg|Combined shrine hall (Hakusan Shrine, Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine) File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Akiba-jinja.jpg|Akiba Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner2.jpg|Subsidiary shrines === Festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}} * Hatsu-uma Festival (early {{ill|lt=February|Q109|WD=Q109|1=February}}) * Tsushima Shrine {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} ({{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121|1=July}}, Marine Day) * Kaze Festival {{ill|lt=September 1|Q2859|WD=Q2859|1=September 1}} * Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine Annual Grand Festival ** Eve Festival: first Saturday of {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124|1=October}} ** Grand Festival: the following Sunday * Shichi-Go-San celebration (mid {{ill|lt=November|Q125|WD=Q125|1=November}}) * New Year’s Eve Festival (late night of {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) === On-site information === ;Address * 847 {{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru|1=Ōmaru}}, {{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847|1=Inagi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}} ;Access * Nearest station: About an 8-minute walk (approx. 600 m south) from {{ill|lt=Minami-Tama Station|Q5366901|WD=Q5366901|1=Minami-Tama Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Nambu Line|Q1366263|WD=Q1366263|1=Nambu Line}} ;Surroundings Located on the northern edge of the central {{ill|lt=Tama Hills|Q1502721|WD=Q1502721|1=Tama Hills}}, the shrine offers a panoramic view from its precincts of the {{ill|lt=Tama River lowland|Q131910796|WD=Q131910796|lt=alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River|1=alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River}}. The wooded hill behind the precincts was formerly called Shiro-yama; in the medieval period, a mountain castle known as Ōmaru Castle, a {{ill|lt=yamajiro|Q15710038|WD=Q15710038|1=Hill castle}}, was built there. Today, the area forms part of the {{ill|lt=Tama New Town|Q7680510|WD=Q7680510|1=Tama New Town}} {{ill|lt=Kōyōdai (Inagi City)|Q11416047|WD=Q11416047|lt=Kōyōdai|1=Kōyōdai}} residential district. Along both sides of the approach to the shrine are the Buddhist temples Enshō-ji and Jikishin-an, and to the north of the precincts is another Buddhist temple, Fumon Zen’an. === Notes === === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Further reading === * {{cite book|language=ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Vol. 95, Tama District no. 7|chapter=Ōmaru Village, Marumiya Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/50}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884–6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Omatonotsunotenjinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:稲城市の建築物]] [[Category:稲城市の歴史]]

'東京都稲城市大丸847', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '東京都青梅市一御岳山', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Omatonotsunoten-jinja (Inagi)}} [[JA:大麻止乃豆乃天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11442032}} == Japanese content == {{Original research|date=December 28, 2016 (Wed) 05:25 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine |Image = [[File:Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (May 2009) |Location = {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847|1=Inagi}}{{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru|1=Ōmaru}} 847 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939|WD=Q108039939|1=Kushimachi no Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} (theoretical identification); former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = The Sunday following the first Saturday of {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124|1=October}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Ōmatono
Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine''' (おおまとのつのてんじんしゃ; also read おおまとのつのあまつかみのやしろ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru|1=Ōmaru}}, {{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847|1=Inagi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main sanctuary * {{ill|lt=Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939|WD=Q108039939|1=Kushimachi no Mikoto}} (くしまちのみこと) : A deity said to be the father of Ame-no-Sokotachi no MikotoJunzaburō Hayakawa, “Watarai-shi Keizu,” *Keizu Sōran*, vol. 2, Kokushokankōkai, 1915, p. 396.. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the entry for the Ame-no-Kagu-yama-ni-masu Kushimachi no Mikoto Shrine in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Amekaguyama Shrine|Q98082987|WD=Q98082987|1=Amanokaguyama Shrine}}, located on the northern foothills of {{ill|lt=Mt. Amekaguyama|Q11443780|WD=Q11443780|1=Mount Amanokagu}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture) includes the note “former name: Ōmatonochi no Kami.” From this, it has been considered that this shrine enshrines the same deity as Amekaguyama Shrine. Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was called Marumiya Myōjin, which is said to be a corruption of “Matono-no-miya Myōjin”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}. === History === [[Image:Torii of Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Torii gate (May 2009)]] [[Image:Komainu in Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Komainu. Inscribed “An auspicious day in the sixth month of the twelfth year of {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}}, {{ill|lt=Kanoto-ushi|Q904956|WD=Q904956|1=Yin Metal Ox}}” ({{ill|lt=1841|Q7622|WD=Q7622|1=1841}}). (May 2009)]] [[Image:View from Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the precincts over the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=alluvial plain|1=UNKNOWN}} of the middle reaches of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}}. {{ill|lt=Inagi Bridge|Q11596545|WD=Q11596545|1=Inagi Bridge}} can be seen spanning the Tama River. (May 2009)]] Although it is a very small shrine and its origins are unclear, the *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Report on Shikinaisha Investigations|1=UNKNOWN}}* identifies this shrine as the “Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine” listed in the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}* for {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789|1=Tama district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}. However, regarding the Shikinaisha Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine, {{ill|lt=Musashi Mitake Shrine|Q6587276|WD=Q6587276|1=Musashi-Mitake Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōme City|Q237683|WD=Q237683|1=Ōme, Tokyo}}, Tokyo, is also considered a {{ill|lt=theoretical identification|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} Musashi Mitake Shrine was known as Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine. The name “Ōmatono-tsu” is thought to mean “a landing place on a large, round, flat plain”Inagi City Board of Education (ed.), *Inagi no Chimei to Kyūdō*, p. 73.. In fact, the former place name of the lowland in front of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fumon Zen’an|1=UNKNOWN}} adjacent to the shrine precincts was “Irigata,” and it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lagoon|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Old Tama River|1=UNKNOWN}} sandwiched between Tenjin-yama and Shiro-yama (which contains remains of a {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} fort). The place name Ōmaru in Inagi City, where this shrine stands, is also said to mean “large, round, flat plain.” This area has been one of the river-crossing points of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}} since ancient times (where the {{ill|lt=Fuchū Highway|Q11486060|WD=Q11486060|1=Fuchū Highway}} and the Kawasaki Highway converge), so it is reasonable to think that there was a {{ill|lt=river port|Q11555884|WD=Q11555884|1=river port}} here. Incidentally, the aforementioned Shiro-yama is also thought to have been built to control the Tama River crossing. === Subsidiary shrines === '''{{ill|lt=Akiba Shrine|Q11595841|WD=Q11595841|1=Akiba Shrines}}'''{{efn|The section on Marumiya Shrine in the *{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}* includes the note “subsidiary shrine: Akiba Shrine”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}.}} :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} '''{{ill|lt=Tsushima Shrine|Q705136|WD=Q705136|1=Tsushima Shrine}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} :[Event] Annual grand festival (Marine Day in July) '''{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}''' × 2 :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} :[Event] Hatsu-uma Festival (early February) '''{{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikuri-hime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (くくりひめのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (いざなぎのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (いざなみのみこと) '''{{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|1=Shinmei shrines}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Tsushima-jinja.jpg|Tsushima Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner1.jpg|Combined shrine hall (Hakusan Shrine, Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine) File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Akiba-jinja.jpg|Akiba Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner2.jpg|Subsidiary shrines === Festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}} * Hatsu-uma Festival (early {{ill|lt=February|Q109|WD=Q109|1=February}}) * Tsushima Shrine {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} ({{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121|1=July}}, Marine Day) * Kaze Festival {{ill|lt=September 1|Q2859|WD=Q2859|1=September 1}} * Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine Annual Grand Festival ** Eve Festival: first Saturday of {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124|1=October}} ** Grand Festival: the following Sunday * Shichi-Go-San celebration (mid {{ill|lt=November|Q125|WD=Q125|1=November}}) * New Year’s Eve Festival (late night of {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) === On-site information === ;Address * 847 {{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru|1=Ōmaru}}, {{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847|1=Inagi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}} ;Access * Nearest station: About an 8-minute walk (approx. 600 m south) from {{ill|lt=Minami-Tama Station|Q5366901|WD=Q5366901|1=Minami-Tama Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Nambu Line|Q1366263|WD=Q1366263|1=Nambu Line}} ;Surroundings Located on the northern edge of the central {{ill|lt=Tama Hills|Q1502721|WD=Q1502721|1=Tama Hills}}, the shrine offers a panoramic view from its precincts of the {{ill|lt=Tama River lowland|Q131910796|WD=Q131910796|lt=alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River|1=alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River}}. The wooded hill behind the precincts was formerly called Shiro-yama; in the medieval period, a mountain castle known as Ōmaru Castle, a {{ill|lt=yamajiro|Q15710038|WD=Q15710038|1=Hill castle}}, was built there. Today, the area forms part of the {{ill|lt=Tama New Town|Q7680510|WD=Q7680510|1=Tama New Town}} {{ill|lt=Kōyōdai (Inagi City)|Q11416047|WD=Q11416047|lt=Kōyōdai|1=Kōyōdai}} residential district. Along both sides of the approach to the shrine are the Buddhist temples Enshō-ji and Jikishin-an, and to the north of the precincts is another Buddhist temple, Fumon Zen’an. === Notes === === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Further reading === * {{cite book|language=ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Vol. 95, Tama District no. 7|chapter=Ōmaru Village, Marumiya Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/50}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884–6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Omatonotsunotenjinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:稲城市の建築物]] [[Category:稲城市の歴史]]

In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the entry for the Ame-no-Kagu-yama-ni-masu Kushimachi no Mikoto Shrine in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Amekaguyama Shrine|Q98082987|WD=Q98082987|1=Amanokaguyama Shrine}}, located on the northern foothills of {{ill|lt=Mt. Amekaguyama|Q11443780|WD=Q11443780|1=Mount Amanokagu}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture) includes the note “former name: Ōmatonochi no Kami.” From this, it has been considered that this shrine enshrines the same deity as Amekaguyama Shrine. Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was called Marumiya Myōjin, which is said to be a corruption of “Matono-no-miya Myōjin”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}. === History === [[Image:Torii of Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Torii gate (May 2009)]] [[Image:Komainu in Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Komainu. Inscribed “An auspicious day in the sixth month of the twelfth year of {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}}, {{ill|lt=Kanoto-ushi|Q904956|WD=Q904956|1=Yin Metal Ox}}” ({{ill|lt=1841|Q7622|WD=Q7622|1=1841}}). (May 2009)]] [[Image:View from Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the precincts over the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=alluvial plain|1=UNKNOWN}} of the middle reaches of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}}. {{ill|lt=Inagi Bridge|Q11596545|WD=Q11596545|1=Inagi Bridge}} can be seen spanning the Tama River. (May 2009)]] Although it is a very small shrine and its origins are unclear, the *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Report on Shikinaisha Investigations|1=UNKNOWN}}* identifies this shrine as the “Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine” listed in the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}* for {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789|1=Tama district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}. However, regarding the Shikinaisha Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine, {{ill|lt=Musashi Mitake Shrine|Q6587276|WD=Q6587276|1=Musashi-Mitake Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōme City|Q237683|WD=Q237683|1=Ōme, Tokyo}}, Tokyo, is also considered a {{ill|lt=theoretical identification|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} Musashi Mitake Shrine was known as Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine. The name “Ōmatono-tsu” is thought to mean “a landing place on a large, round, flat plain”Inagi City Board of Education (ed.), *Inagi no Chimei to Kyūdō*, p. 73.. In fact, the former place name of the lowland in front of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fumon Zen’an|1=UNKNOWN}} adjacent to the shrine precincts was “Irigata,” and it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lagoon|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Old Tama River|1=UNKNOWN}} sandwiched between Tenjin-yama and Shiro-yama (which contains remains of a {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} fort). The place name Ōmaru in Inagi City, where this shrine stands, is also said to mean “large, round, flat plain.” This area has been one of the river-crossing points of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}} since ancient times (where the {{ill|lt=Fuchū Highway|Q11486060|WD=Q11486060|1=Fuchū Highway}} and the Kawasaki Highway converge), so it is reasonable to think that there was a {{ill|lt=river port|Q11555884|WD=Q11555884|1=river port}} here. Incidentally, the aforementioned Shiro-yama is also thought to have been built to control the Tama River crossing. === Subsidiary shrines === '''{{ill|lt=Akiba Shrine|Q11595841|WD=Q11595841|1=Akiba Shrines}}'''{{efn|The section on Marumiya Shrine in the *{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}* includes the note “subsidiary shrine: Akiba Shrine”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}.}} :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} '''{{ill|lt=Tsushima Shrine|Q705136|WD=Q705136|1=Tsushima Shrine}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} :[Event] Annual grand festival (Marine Day in July) '''{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}''' × 2 :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} :[Event] Hatsu-uma Festival (early February) '''{{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikuri-hime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (くくりひめのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (いざなぎのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (いざなみのみこと) '''{{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|1=Shinmei shrines}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Tsushima-jinja.jpg|Tsushima Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner1.jpg|Combined shrine hall (Hakusan Shrine, Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine) File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Akiba-jinja.jpg|Akiba Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner2.jpg|Subsidiary shrines === Festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}} * Hatsu-uma Festival (early {{ill|lt=February|Q109|WD=Q109|1=February}}) * Tsushima Shrine {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} ({{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121|1=July}}, Marine Day) * Kaze Festival {{ill|lt=September 1|Q2859|WD=Q2859|1=September 1}} * Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine Annual Grand Festival ** Eve Festival: first Saturday of {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124|1=October}} ** Grand Festival: the following Sunday * Shichi-Go-San celebration (mid {{ill|lt=November|Q125|WD=Q125|1=November}}) * New Year’s Eve Festival (late night of {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) === On-site information === ;Address * 847 {{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru|1=Ōmaru}}, {{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847|1=Inagi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}} ;Access * Nearest station: About an 8-minute walk (approx. 600 m south) from {{ill|lt=Minami-Tama Station|Q5366901|WD=Q5366901|1=Minami-Tama Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Nambu Line|Q1366263|WD=Q1366263|1=Nambu Line}} ;Surroundings Located on the northern edge of the central {{ill|lt=Tama Hills|Q1502721|WD=Q1502721|1=Tama Hills}}, the shrine offers a panoramic view from its precincts of the {{ill|lt=Tama River lowland|Q131910796|WD=Q131910796|lt=alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River|1=alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River}}. The wooded hill behind the precincts was formerly called Shiro-yama; in the medieval period, a mountain castle known as Ōmaru Castle, a {{ill|lt=yamajiro|Q15710038|WD=Q15710038|1=Hill castle}}, was built there. Today, the area forms part of the {{ill|lt=Tama New Town|Q7680510|WD=Q7680510|1=Tama New Town}} {{ill|lt=Kōyōdai (Inagi City)|Q11416047|WD=Q11416047|lt=Kōyōdai|1=Kōyōdai}} residential district. Along both sides of the approach to the shrine are the Buddhist temples Enshō-ji and Jikishin-an, and to the north of the precincts is another Buddhist temple, Fumon Zen’an. === Notes === === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Further reading === * {{cite book|language=ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Vol. 95, Tama District no. 7|chapter=Ōmaru Village, Marumiya Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/50}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884–6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:稲城市の建築物]] [[Category:稲城市の歴史]]

Amekaguyama|Q11443780|WD=Q11443780|1=Mount Amanokagu}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture) includes the note “former name: Ōmatonochi no Kami.” From this, it has been considered that this shrine enshrines the same deity as Amekaguyama Shrine. Incidentally, the aforementioned Shiro-yama is also thought to have been built to control the Tama River crossing. === Subsidiary shrines === '''{{ill|lt=Akiba Shrine|Q11595841|WD=Q11595841|1=Akiba Shrines}}'''{{efn|The section on Marumiya Shrine in the *{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}* includes the note “subsidiary shrine: Akiba Shrine”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}.}} :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} '''{{ill|lt=Tsushima Shrine|Q705136|WD=Q705136|1=Tsushima Shrine}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} :[Event] Annual grand festival (Marine Day in July) '''{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}''' × 2 :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} :[Event] Hatsu-uma Festival (early February) '''{{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikuri-hime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (くくりひめのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (いざなぎのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (いざなみのみこと) '''{{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|1=Shinmei shrines}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Tsushima-jinja.jpg|Tsushima Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner1.jpg|Combined shrine hall (Hakusan Shrine, Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine) File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Akiba-jinja.jpg|Akiba Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner2.jpg|Subsidiary shrines === Festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}} * Hatsu-uma Festival (early {{ill|lt=February|Q109|WD=Q109|1=February}}) * Tsushima Shrine {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} ({{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121|1=July}}, Marine Day) * Kaze Festival {{ill|lt=September 1|Q2859|WD=Q2859|1=September 1}} * Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine Annual Grand Festival ** Eve Festival: first Saturday of {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124|1=October}} ** Grand Festival: the following Sunday * Shichi-Go-San celebration (mid {{ill|lt=November|Q125|WD=Q125|1=November}}) * New Year’s Eve Festival (late night of {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) === On-site information === ;Address * 847 {{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru|1=Ōmaru}}, {{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847|1=Inagi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}} ;Access * Nearest station: About an 8-minute walk (approx. 600 m south) from {{ill|lt=Minami-Tama Station|Q5366901|WD=Q5366901|1=Minami-Tama Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Nambu Line|Q1366263|WD=Q1366263|1=Nambu Line}} ;Surroundings Located on the northern edge of the central {{ill|lt=Tama Hills|Q1502721|WD=Q1502721|1=Tama Hills}}, the shrine offers a panoramic view from its precincts of the {{ill|lt=Tama River lowland|Q131910796|WD=Q131910796|lt=alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River|1=alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River}}. The wooded hill behind the precincts was formerly called Shiro-yama; in the medieval period, a mountain castle known as Ōmaru Castle, a {{ill|lt=yamajiro|Q15710038|WD=Q15710038|1=Hill castle}}, was built there. Today, the area forms part of the {{ill|lt=Tama New Town|Q7680510|WD=Q7680510|1=Tama New Town}} {{ill|lt=Kōyōdai (Inagi City)|Q11416047|WD=Q11416047|lt=Kōyōdai|1=Kōyōdai}} residential district. Along both sides of the approach to the shrine are the Buddhist temples Enshō-ji and Jikishin-an, and to the north of the precincts is another Buddhist temple, Fumon Zen’an. === Notes === === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Further reading === * {{cite book|language=ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Vol. 95, Tama District no. 7|chapter=Ōmaru Village, Marumiya Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/50}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884–6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:稲城市の建築物]] [[Category:稲城市の歴史]]

== {{Original research|date=December 28, 2016 (Wed) 05:25 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine |Image = [[File:Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (May 2009) |Location = {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847|1=Inagi}}{{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru|1=Ōmaru}} 847 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939|WD=Q108039939|1=Kushimachi no Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} (theoretical identification); former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = The Sunday following the first Saturday of {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124|1=October}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Ōmatono
Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine''' (おおまとのつのてんじんしゃ; also read おおまとのつのあまつかみのやしろ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru|1=Ōmaru}}, {{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847|1=Inagi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main sanctuary * {{ill|lt=Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939|WD=Q108039939|1=Kushimachi no Mikoto}} (くしまちのみこと) : A deity said to be the father of Ame-no-Sokotachi no MikotoJunzaburō Hayakawa, “Watarai-shi Keizu,” *Keizu Sōran*, vol. 2, Kokushokankōkai, 1915, p. 396.. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the entry for the Ame-no-Kagu-yama-ni-masu Kushimachi no Mikoto Shrine in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Amekaguyama Shrine|Q98082987|WD=Q98082987|1=Amanokaguyama Shrine}}, located on the northern foothills of {{ill|lt=Mt. Amekaguyama|Q11443780|WD=Q11443780|1=Mount Amanokagu}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture) includes the note “former name: Ōmatonochi no Kami.” From this, it has been considered that this shrine enshrines the same deity as Amekaguyama Shrine. Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was called Marumiya Myōjin, which is said to be a corruption of “Matono-no-miya Myōjin”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}. === History === [[Image:Torii of Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Torii gate (May 2009)]] [[Image:Komainu in Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Komainu. Inscribed “An auspicious day in the sixth month of the twelfth year of {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}}, {{ill|lt=Kanoto-ushi|Q904956|WD=Q904956|1=Yin Metal Ox}}” ({{ill|lt=1841|Q7622|WD=Q7622|1=1841}}). (May 2009)]] [[Image:View from Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the precincts over the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=alluvial plain|1=UNKNOWN}} of the middle reaches of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}}. {{ill|lt=Inagi Bridge|Q11596545|WD=Q11596545|1=Inagi Bridge}} can be seen spanning the Tama River. (May 2009)]] Although it is a very small shrine and its origins are unclear, the *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Report on Shikinaisha Investigations|1=UNKNOWN}}* identifies this shrine as the “Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine” listed in the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}* for {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789|1=Tama district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}. However, regarding the Shikinaisha Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine, {{ill|lt=Musashi Mitake Shrine|Q6587276|WD=Q6587276|1=Musashi-Mitake Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōme City|Q237683|WD=Q237683|1=Ōme, Tokyo}}, Tokyo, is also considered a {{ill|lt=theoretical identification|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} Musashi Mitake Shrine was known as Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine. The name “Ōmatono-tsu” is thought to mean “a landing place on a large, round, flat plain”Inagi City Board of Education (ed.), *Inagi no Chimei to Kyūdō*, p. 73.. In fact, the former place name of the lowland in front of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fumon Zen’an|1=UNKNOWN}} adjacent to the shrine precincts was “Irigata,” and it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lagoon|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Old Tama River|1=UNKNOWN}} sandwiched between Tenjin-yama and Shiro-yama (which contains remains of a {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} fort). The place name Ōmaru in Inagi City, where this shrine stands, is also said to mean “large, round, flat plain.” This area has been one of the river-crossing points of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}} since ancient times (where the {{ill|lt=Fuchū Highway|Q11486060|WD=Q11486060|1=Fuchū Highway}} and the Kawasaki Highway converge), so it is reasonable to think that there was a {{ill|lt=river port|Q11555884|WD=Q11555884|1=river port}} here. Incidentally, the aforementioned Shiro-yama is also thought to have been built to control the Tama River crossing. === Subsidiary shrines === '''{{ill|lt=Akiba Shrine|Q11595841|WD=Q11595841|1=Akiba Shrines}}'''{{efn|The section on Marumiya Shrine in the *{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}* includes the note “subsidiary shrine: Akiba Shrine”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}.}} :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} '''{{ill|lt=Tsushima Shrine|Q705136|WD=Q705136|1=Tsushima Shrine}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} :[Event] Annual grand festival (Marine Day in July) '''{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}''' × 2 :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} :[Event] Hatsu-uma Festival (early February) '''{{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikuri-hime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (くくりひめのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (いざなぎのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (いざなみのみこと) '''{{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|1=Shinmei shrines}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|lt=Nambu Line|Q1366263|WD=Q1366263|1=Nambu Line}} ;Surroundings Located on the northern edge of the central {{ill|lt=Tama Hills|Q1502721|WD=Q1502721|1=Tama Hills}}, the shrine offers a panoramic view from its precincts of the {{ill|lt=Tama River lowland|Q131910796|WD=Q131910796|lt=alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River|1=alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River}}. The wooded hill behind the precincts was formerly called Shiro-yama; in the medieval period, a mountain castle known as Ōmaru Castle, a {{ill|lt=yamajiro|Q15710038|WD=Q15710038|1=Hill castle}}, was built there. Today, the area forms part of the {{ill|lt=Tama New Town|Q7680510|WD=Q7680510|1=Tama New Town}} {{ill|lt=Kōyōdai (Inagi City)|Q11416047|WD=Q11416047|lt=Kōyōdai|1=Kōyōdai}} residential district. Along both sides of the approach to the shrine are the Buddhist temples Enshō-ji and Jikishin-an, and to the north of the precincts is another Buddhist temple, Fumon Zen’an. === Notes === === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Further reading === * {{cite book|language=ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Vol. 95, Tama District no. 7|chapter=Ōmaru Village, Marumiya Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/50}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884–6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:稲城市の建築物]] [[Category:稲城市の歴史]]

== {{Original research|date=December 28, 2016 (Wed) 05:25 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine |Image = [[File:Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (May 2009) |Location = {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847|1=Inagi}}{{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru|1=Ōmaru}} 847 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939|WD=Q108039939|1=Kushimachi no Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} (theoretical identification); former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other name = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = The Sunday following the first Saturday of {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124|1=October}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo|label=Ōmatono
Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine''' (おおまとのつのてんじんしゃ; also read おおまとのつのあまつかみのやしろ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru|1=Ōmaru}}, {{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847|1=Inagi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main sanctuary * {{ill|lt=Kushimachi no Mikoto|Q108039939|WD=Q108039939|1=Kushimachi no Mikoto}} (くしまちのみこと) : A deity said to be the father of Ame-no-Sokotachi no MikotoJunzaburō Hayakawa, “Watarai-shi Keizu,” *Keizu Sōran*, vol. 2, Kokushokankōkai, 1915, p. 396.. In the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, the entry for the Ame-no-Kagu-yama-ni-masu Kushimachi no Mikoto Shrine in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (present-day {{ill|lt=Amekaguyama Shrine|Q98082987|WD=Q98082987|1=Amanokaguyama Shrine}}, located on the northern foothills of {{ill|lt=Mt. Amekaguyama|Q11443780|WD=Q11443780|1=Mount Amanokagu}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture) includes the note “former name: Ōmatonochi no Kami.” From this, it has been considered that this shrine enshrines the same deity as Amekaguyama Shrine. Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was called Marumiya Myōjin, which is said to be a corruption of “Matono-no-miya Myōjin”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}. === History === [[Image:Torii of Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Torii gate (May 2009)]] [[Image:Komainu in Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|Komainu. Inscribed “An auspicious day in the sixth month of the twelfth year of {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}}, {{ill|lt=Kanoto-ushi|Q904956|WD=Q904956|1=Yin Metal Ox}}” ({{ill|lt=1841|Q7622|WD=Q7622|1=1841}}). (May 2009)]] [[Image:View from Oomatono Tsunoten Shinto Shrine in Inagi taken in May 2009.jpg|thumb|180px|View from the precincts over the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=alluvial plain|1=UNKNOWN}} of the middle reaches of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}}. {{ill|lt=Inagi Bridge|Q11596545|WD=Q11596545|1=Inagi Bridge}} can be seen spanning the Tama River. (May 2009)]] Although it is a very small shrine and its origins are unclear, the *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Report on Shikinaisha Investigations|1=UNKNOWN}}* identifies this shrine as the “Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine” listed in the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}* for {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|WD=Q11430789|1=Tama district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}. However, regarding the Shikinaisha Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine, {{ill|lt=Musashi Mitake Shrine|Q6587276|WD=Q6587276|1=Musashi-Mitake Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōme City|Q237683|WD=Q237683|1=Ōme, Tokyo}}, Tokyo, is also considered a {{ill|lt=theoretical identification|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} Musashi Mitake Shrine was known as Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine. The name “Ōmatono-tsu” is thought to mean “a landing place on a large, round, flat plain”Inagi City Board of Education (ed.), *Inagi no Chimei to Kyūdō*, p. 73.. In fact, the former place name of the lowland in front of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fumon Zen’an|1=UNKNOWN}} adjacent to the shrine precincts was “Irigata,” and it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lagoon|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Old Tama River|1=UNKNOWN}} sandwiched between Tenjin-yama and Shiro-yama (which contains remains of a {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} fort). The place name Ōmaru in Inagi City, where this shrine stands, is also said to mean “large, round, flat plain.” This area has been one of the river-crossing points of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|WD=Q1063006|1=Tama River}} since ancient times (where the {{ill|lt=Fuchū Highway|Q11486060|WD=Q11486060|1=Fuchū Highway}} and the Kawasaki Highway converge), so it is reasonable to think that there was a {{ill|lt=river port|Q11555884|WD=Q11555884|1=river port}} here. Incidentally, the aforementioned Shiro-yama is also thought to have been built to control the Tama River crossing. === Subsidiary shrines === '''{{ill|lt=Akiba Shrine|Q11595841|WD=Q11595841|1=Akiba Shrines}}'''{{efn|The section on Marumiya Shrine in the *{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}* includes the note “subsidiary shrine: Akiba Shrine”{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}.}} :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} '''{{ill|lt=Tsushima Shrine|Q705136|WD=Q705136|1=Tsushima Shrine}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takesusanoo no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} :[Event] Annual grand festival (Marine Day in July) '''{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}''' × 2 :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} :[Event] Hatsu-uma Festival (early February) '''{{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikuri-hime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (くくりひめのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (いざなぎのみこと) :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (いざなみのみこと) '''{{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|1=Shinmei shrines}}''' :[Enshrined deity] {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Tsushima-jinja.jpg|Tsushima Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner1.jpg|Combined shrine hall (Hakusan Shrine, Shinmei Shrine, Inari Shrine) File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Akiba-jinja.jpg|Akiba Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Omatonotsunoten-jinja(Inagi) Inner2.jpg|Subsidiary shrines === Festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}} * Hatsu-uma Festival (early {{ill|lt=February|Q109|WD=Q109|1=February}}) * Tsushima Shrine {{ill|lt=annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} ({{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121|1=July}}, Marine Day) * Kaze Festival {{ill|lt=September 1|Q2859|WD=Q2859|1=September 1}} * Ōmatono Tsunoten Tenjin Shrine Annual Grand Festival ** Eve Festival: first Saturday of {{ill|lt=October|Q124|WD=Q124|1=October}} ** Grand Festival: the following Sunday * Shichi-Go-San celebration (mid {{ill|lt=November|Q125|WD=Q125|1=November}}) * New Year’s Eve Festival (late night of {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) === On-site information === ;Address * 847 {{ill|lt=Ōmaru (Inagi City)|Q30923766|WD=Q30923766|lt=Ōmaru|1=Ōmaru}}, {{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|WD=Q317847|1=Inagi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}} ;Access * Nearest station: About an 8-minute walk (approx. 600 m south) from {{ill|lt=Minami-Tama Station|Q5366901|WD=Q5366901|1=Minami-Tama Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Nambu Line|Q1366263|WD=Q1366263|1=Nambu Line}} ;Surroundings Located on the northern edge of the central {{ill|lt=Tama Hills|Q1502721|WD=Q1502721|1=Tama Hills}}, the shrine offers a panoramic view from its precincts of the {{ill|lt=Tama River lowland|Q131910796|WD=Q131910796|lt=alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River|1=alluvial plain of the middle reaches of the Tama River}}. The wooded hill behind the precincts was formerly called Shiro-yama; in the medieval period, a mountain castle known as Ōmaru Castle, a {{ill|lt=yamajiro|Q15710038|WD=Q15710038|1=Hill castle}}, was built there. Today, the area forms part of the {{ill|lt=Tama New Town|Q7680510|WD=Q7680510|1=Tama New Town}} {{ill|lt=Kōyōdai (Inagi City)|Q11416047|WD=Q11416047|lt=Kōyōdai|1=Kōyōdai}} residential district. Along both sides of the approach to the shrine are the Buddhist temples Enshō-ji and Jikishin-an, and to the north of the precincts is another Buddhist temple, Fumon Zen’an. === Notes === === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === Further reading === * {{cite book|language=ja|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|volume=Vol. 95, Tama District no. 7|chapter=Ōmaru Village, Marumiya Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/50}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884–6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō Ōmaru Village}}}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmatonotsunoten Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:稲城市の建築物]] [[Category:稲城市の歴史]]
